“We humbly acknowledged the non-reversal of CIP and Accounts Payable (AP) for the noted 2 ongoing projects and we promise to strictly monitor our accruals at the end of the year. Adjustments were already made as of February 28, 2025 financial reports totaling ₱21,256,291.22 thru JEV No. 100-1106 & 100-1107 pertaining to Project -3 Storey Multi-Purpose Building & JEV 100-1118 pertaining to Project – 2 Storey 8CL@ Dalig Kalinga Elementary School.
Further, the amount of completed project – Improvement of RHU located at Municipal Compound Aurora, Isabela was also missed to be classified to PPE during the year. Adjusting entry is prepared and reflected this March closing of the books thru JEV 100-1443 amounting ₱706,013.28.”

And as to the noted 2 on-going projects namely, Construction of Gymnasium in Brgy. Sili and Rehabilitation of Roads-Brgy. Bagong Tanza with minimal work performed from December 19 to 27, 2024 adjusting entry is prepared by recording CIP and AP based on Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) prepared and certified by the Municipal Engineering Office reflected this March closing thru JEV 300-077 and 100-1441.”

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The actions taken by the management to correct the deficiencies noted after the balance sheet date are therefore duly acknowledged. The Audit Team shall continue to monitor and evaluate LGU’s compliance on similar transactions for the succeeding audit year.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management oversee the adherence to the pertinent provisions of NGAS Manual for LGUs and applicable policies and guidelines on the accounting and management of inventory to ensure the reliability and fair presentation of the balances in the financial statements.
In particular, we recommended that Management direct:
The Municipal Accountant to:
Adopt/use the weighted average method in the valuation of inventory items; and
Institute measures to ensure that inventory items received as donation are properly recorded and accounted for in the books;
The Inventory Committee to undertake complete physical inventory every six months, as required by the Manual, and submit the RPCI in order to ascertain the correctness of the inventory;
The GSO and Accounting Office to properly maintain and update SCs and SLCs, respectively, for all inventory items to facilitate the review, physical count, and reconciliation of inventories; and
The GSO to prepare the SSMI as prescribed in NGAS Manual for LGUs. Upon receipt of the report, the Accounting Office shall fill up the unit cost and total cost in the SSMI, which will serve as the basis for recording the expenditure in the books.
Further, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Health Officer and heads of all other concerned department who are receiving goods-in-kind from National Government Agencies or from any source, to inform and coordinate with the GSO of such goods, before issuance to the concerned department or end-user. The GSO shall thereafter furnish the accounting department the necessary documents on the receipt and issuance for proper recording in the books of accounts.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant, draw the appropriate adjusting journal entry to correct the improper amortization of the insurance for buildings and motor vehicles in order to accurately reflect the balances of the affected accounts.
Henceforth, conduct periodic reviews and reconciliations of the Prepaid Insurance account and ensure that all policies are properly amortized within their respective coverage periods in accordance with IPSAS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute and adopt additional system of internal control in safeguarding the LGU’s assets, particularly Cash and direct the Accounting and Cashier Sections to process payments of allowances and other personnel benefits directly to the Municipal officials and employees through their bank’s ATMs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
Strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and
Observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team by setting timelines of submission from the Treasury Office to the Accounting Office. Likewise, maintain processing and payment of transactions with complete supporting documents. Without doing so, delays will affect both the Treasury Office and the Accounting Office on submission of accounts.

We, therefore, recommended that the management ensure full compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022, as supplemented by COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024. Specifically, classify all purchased and/or issued semi-expendables to their appropriate accounts and prepare and maintain all applicable prescribed forms, reports, and registries.

We, therefore, recommended that the management provide a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) or contract approved by the LGU and other relevant authorities and a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution explicitly authorizing the use of the involved units to the recipient. Otherwise, provide a written explanation of why that said properties were issued to a private individual.

Moving forward, the management, under the leadership of the LCE, ensure that government properties serve public rather than private interests and that government assets are duly accounted for and safeguarded, ascertaining they are not misused or unaccounted for in inventory reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Exert effort to determine the nature of the unremitted prior period balances and effect immediately their remittance. Thereafter, ensure the faithful, regular, and full remittances of mandatory deductions in compliance with the requirements and deadlines set by the respective agencies; and
Observe consistency in the disclosures presented in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSASs) No. 1.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), through its secretariat, submit all contracts, together with all the supporting documents forming part thereof, within five (5) days from its perfection to the Auditor to enable the latter to conduct auditorial review, before these contracts are forwarded to COA Regional Office for technical evaluation, without prejudice to the penal clause cited under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
In addition, we also recommended that Management assess the workload of the personnel in charge of the submission of contract documents, ensuring greater efficiency in the delivery of public services. This assessment should also help determine if there is a need for additional personnel to support this function.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue her effort in complying with the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements for fair presentation of Cash in Bank accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended that management require:
The contractor to properly scatter the aggregates as required in the 1st project in compliance with Section 62.2.2 of the procurement law; and
The immediate reconstruction of the pavement while taking solutions to mitigate the flood in said area.
Also, management was recommended to observe the provisions of Section 37.4.1 of R.A. No. 9184 as to the effectivity of the Notices to Proceed in order to provide the correct dates of contract implementation.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of the code especially on when and how to issue variation orders as provided under Item 1.1, Annex E of R.A. No. 9184 highlighting that addition/deletion of works under variation order should be within the general scope of the project as bid and awarded.
Also, management was recommended to observe the provisions of Section 37.4.1 of R.A. No. 9184 as to the effectivity of the Notice to Proceed in order to provide the correct dates of contract implementation.

We recommended that the Management require the:
Relative to the Concreting of Road Barangay San Ramon, management furnished the contractor a copy of the said AOM and informed him regarding the observation and immediately carried out the rectification of the defects in order to maintain the durability of the pavement.
Relative to the Construction of Aurora Eco-Tourism Park Phase II located at Sili, Aurora, Isabela; Construction of Cattle House Phase II located at Macatal, Aurora, Isabela, and Construction of Barangay Health Center Station (BHS) with Motor Pump & Water Tank located at Macatal, Aurora, Isabela, they submitted related documents such as Request for Suspension, Resume of Works and Request for Time Extension to support that the project was completed on time based on the justifications provided by the contractor.
For the Construction of One (1) Building Public Market (10m x 34.2m) Stall Section, they submitted related documents such as Request for Suspension, Resume of Works and Request for Work Suspension due to Variation Order and Sangguniang Resolution No. 22-A dated April 18, 2022 approving variation order in the form of change order from the plan and specification of one (1) public market building-stall section to construction of open space public market building to support that the project was completed based on the justification provided by the contractor.
Furthermore, as per Statement of Contract Duration, the project incurred a delay of twelve (12) calendar days subject to the imposition of Liquidated Damages. Rest assured that said Liquidated Damages be deducted from final billing.
Relative to the Construction of One (1) Unit Day Care Center/Child Development Center at Macatal, Aurora, Isabela, aside from submission of related documents such as Request for Suspension, and Request for Resume of Works to support that the project was completed based on the justifications provided by the contractor,  the recommendation to settle the deficiency amounting to One Thousand Six Hundred Fifty Pesos (₱1,650.00) due to non-installation of three (3) faucets was settled by the Inspectorate Team. The Official Receipts and pictures of the deficiency were submitted per LGU’s follow-up reply dated February 26, 2025.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed
through the Municipal Treasurer, Acting Property Custodian, and
concerned offices to (a) register with the Land Transportation Office
the motor vehicle with expired registration and comply with the
provision of R.A 4136; and (b) speed up the disposal of
unserviceable properties in accordance with the revised manual on the disposal of
government properties.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Municipal
Assessor to (i) reconcile the Municipality’s RPT records with the
records of the Provincial Assessor’s Office; (ii) expedite the updating of
records on real property; and (iii) submit to the Municipal Treasurer
on or before the 31st day of December each year the updated
assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties
have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such
properties; (b) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
reconcile and adjust records on RPT accordingly; and (c) Municipal
Treasurer to ensure that it complies with the provisions of Section 254
of R.A No. 7160 on tax delinquencies.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the concerned officials
to (a) carefully examine the PPAs to be included in the appropriation
ordinance to ensure that the same will not include reiterated projects;
(b) carry out the twelve unimplemented projects in CY 2025; and (c)
meticulously plan the implementation of PPAs to improve efficiency in
the utilization of the 20% DF in the next budget years.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the concerned officials
to properly utilize the LDRRMF and exercise prudence in allocating
funds chargeable against the 5% calamity fund.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to sustain the Municipality’s
satisfactory performance in doing its role as partner in the
implementation of national government projects with the view of
promoting efficient utilization of government funds. Nonetheless, we
suggest that Management look into the complaint of the residents of
Barangay Buhangin and implement the necessary corrective actions to
prevent the spread of dengue.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the GDFS to
continuously comply with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9710 and
its Implementing Rules and Regulations, and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously withhold taxes
from employees’ compensations and payments to suppliers, and remit
the same to the BIR in accordance with existing revenue regulations.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to advise the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the
existing rules and regulation on the withholding and remittance of
GSIS contributions and loan amortizations due from employees
including the mandatory employer’s share.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the
rules and regulation on the withholding of HDMF contributions and
loan amortizations from its employees’ salaries, and remittance thereof
including the mandatory employer’s share in a timely manner.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the
existing rules and regulation on the withholding of PhilHealth
contributions from its employees, including the timely remittance

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (a) continue the good practice of diligently review of the
fiscal transactions of the Municipality; and (b) ensure compliance with
whatever decision would be accorded on the disallowances appealed
before COA.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed, through the (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, to exert greater efforts in resolving the disparities in cash balances reflected in the cash book, GLs, and bank statements; and make the necessary adjustments in their respective books, as warranted; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) cancel the stale checks and revert the corresponding amount to CIB and Accounts Payable balance; (ii) take advantage of the availability of bank snapshots in the initial preparation of bank statements to enable its timely submission; (iii) prepare and submit all the BRS for all bank accounts; and (iv) assign the reconciliation task to a competent and permanent employee to promote continuous and uninterrupted preparation of bank reconciliation statements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Accountant to (a) conduct reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and make the necessary adjustments; and (b) henceforth ensure accuracy in the recording of related financial transactions.

We recommended and the LCE to cause the Municipal Treasury Office to (a) set-up controls that would allow an effective monitoring of the receivables from RPT/SET; (b) intensify the campaigns for the collection of delinquent RPT/SET; (c) consider the enforcement of the remedies enumerated by the Local Government Code in cases of tax delinquency.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Treasurer to (a) sustain its satisfactory performance in collecting business taxes; (b) conduct regular business tax mapping; (c) follow-up on terminated businesses which fail to pay the appropriate business taxes.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Accountant to (a) issue demand letters to officials and employees with outstanding CAs; (b) consider the adoption of legal remedies, as warranted, to enforce settlement of CAs; and (c) strictly monitor and enforce the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Municipal Planning and Development Office to strengthen project planning processes to ensure viability of the projects with the view of minimizing, if not eliminating the existence of idle funds; (b) MEO to closely supervise project implementation to prevent execution issues and avoid delays in project completion; and (c) Accounting Office to maintain separate records for special accounts for proper monitoring of projects implemented under this fund.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Municipal Accountant to trace the entries made to recognize the STF during previous years, comply with the required accounting entries in the recording of STF, and journalize the necessary adjusting entries, as warranted; and (b) MDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records in order to arrive at the correct balance of the STF.

We recommended and the LCE through the (a) MENRO and MPDC to prioritize the construction of the SLF in accordance with the SWM plan of the Municipality; and (b) Local Finance Committee to facilitate the procurement of suitable land and equipment essential to the Municipality’s SWM initiatives.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the MSWDO to (a) maintain its GAD plan integration in their regular activities coupled with compliance with existing GAD laws, policies, rules; and (b) continue addressing gender issues by ensuring gender-responsive Programs, Activities and Projects.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the MHRMO and Municipal Accounting Office to (i) coordinate in classifying the unidentified amounts that form part of the unremitted balances at year-end; and (ii) henceforth endeavor to strictly observe the provisions of RMC No. 23-2012.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the MHRMO and Municipal Accounting Office to (i) collaborate in identifying the details of the unremitted balance of its Due to GSIS account, determine its accuracy and act accordingly; and (ii) remit the long outstanding underpayment to GSIS as mentioned in the demand letter.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) MHRMO and Municipal Accounting Office to collaborate in identifying the details of the year-end balance of the Due to PhilHealth account; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) make representations with PhilHealth on how to settle the said balance; and (ii) and make the appropriate remittance, as warranted.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) MHRMO to (i) coordinate with Pag-IBIG to facilitate the issuance of the Pag-IBIG numbers of employees concerned; and (ii) instruct the members to regularly update their loan records; and (b) Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation with Pag-IBIG to ascertain the correctness of the account balance, and take appropriate action for discrepancies, if any.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide justifications for the reported delay by providing a Statement of Project Duration for submission to the Regional Office. Direct those concerned employees to exercise due care and diligence in the inspection and evaluation of projects turned over by the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and/or the Municipal Budget Officer to create a separate Responsibility Center (RC) for their GFPS to facilitate the generation of all reports pertaining to all GAD related financial transactions and to comply the requirements set forth in the COA Circular No. 2021-008 and shall use this RC in the recording of all GAD related transactions.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the GFPS to attend GAD seminars and trainings to update their knowledge and build their competencies in the execution of GAD related laws and regulations, including the concept and application of GMEF/GeRL in determining the LGU’s level of GAD mainstreaming and identifying steps to improve its GAD mainstreaming efforts.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to review available records and documents to verify the deposit made by the Municipal Treasurer and once confirmed for the former to record the transaction to correct the balance of the Cash Local Treasury account.

a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all of its serviceable properties with the GSIS, and follow the guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2018-002; and

b. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide appropriation for the premiums of such insurance coverage with the GSIS. In case of budget constraints, the MBO may charge such appropriation to the LDRRM Fund of the LGU.

A. Issue an executive order creating the CPES – IU, identifying its members and responsibilities or integrate the CPEs function in an existing unit with similar functions which shall act as the CPES – IU; and

B. Formulate and adopt specific Implementing Guidelines for the evaluation of project types which do not have specific CPES Guidelines.

MEEO prepare a 5-Year Business Plan (BP) for each existing LEE in order to determine whether each LEE is still operative and income-generating, or should instead be advised for service shedding, closure, or divestment. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare Financial Reports for each LEE, separate and distinct from the GF.

a) Provide notice of deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from deliveries of goods/items delivered; and 

b) Submit Report of Inspection of Consumables within twenty-four (24) hours.

a) Impose liquidated damages to the concerned contractor; 

b) Forfeit the contractor’s performance security and takeover the prosecution of the project or award the same to a qualified contractor through negotiated contract; and
Require the defaulting AO to restitute immediately the total amount of ₱1.027 million; and to submit the 2891 pcs. unaccounted Accountable Forms and its RCDs.

We recommend that Management: a.) Require the contractor to complete/rectify the deficiencies noted in the project as required in the contract agreement within ten days from receipt of the letter otherwise, the equivalent amount of the unperformed work item in the program of work shall be disallowed in audit; b.) In case the contractor fails/refuses to comply with the order of the Local Chief Executive to undertake completion, LGU shall undertake such completion works and ask for reimbursement from the contractor pursuant to Section 62.2.2.1 of Revised IRR of RA 9184. Otherwise, apply Section 62.2.2.2 of the same IRR; and c.) Direct those concerned employees to exercise due care and diligence in the inspection and evaluation of projects turned over by the contractor.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer provide justifications of the reported delay by providing Statement of Project Duration for submission to the Regional Office. Otherwise, show proof of payment for the peso-equivalent of the nine (9) days Liquidated Damages from the contractor duly supported and “Computation of Liquidated Damages” from his Office.
We recommend that management conduct immediate repair on the longitudinal crack on concrete pavement of the project to prevent further damage and deterioration of the mentioned project.

We, therefore recommend that Management always ensure the ear tagging of Breeding Stocks for inventory control purposes and to ensure the proper handling and management hereof. It is likewise recommended the installation of sound internal control in order to prevent loss thru proper identification of Breeding Stocks.
We recommend that management fix the project's defects in the concrete pavement right away in order to stop its additional damage and deterioration.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer provide justifications of the reported delay by providing Statement of Project Duration for submission to the Regional Office. Otherwise, liquidated damages shall be imposed.
We recommend that management fix the damaged portion of the fence right away in order to stop its additional damages and deterioration.

We recommend that management submit the lacking documents for Item Nos. 2, 3, 9, 11, 12,14, 30 to 35 and 37 as presented in Appendix A hereof for audit evaluation. We further recommend that the Local Finance Committee and the Local Sanggunian be judicious enough to validate the sources of funds for augmentation in case of deficiency for current appropriations as prescribed under Section 336 of RA No. 7160. In case of supplemental budget, which may be funded from (1) new revenue source (2) actual collection exceeded estimated income (3) savings (4) realignment in case of public calamity and (5) reversion, shall be available for re-appropriation to any item of expenditure under any expense class under Section 321 of the same code and subject to review by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan pursuant to Sections 56 and 327 of RA No. 7160. Henceforth, the provisions of Article 417, as amended by AO No. 47 dated April 12, 1993 implementing Section 321 (Changes in Annual Budget, Supplemental Budgets) of RA No. 7160 and Articles 418 and 454 of the IRR of RA No. 7160 implementing Sections 322 (Reversions) and 336 (Augmentation) of RA No. 7160 should be strictly observed.

We recommend that management submit the following: 1. Updated Revenue Code and Ordinance showing revision on the collection of monthly market rentals for holdees; 2. Documents such as Memorandum of Agreements, rental contracts, etc. explaining the Built, Operate and Transfer Scheme to assist the Audit Team in the conduct of audit evaluation; and 3. Reconciliation of the List of Buildings stated in the Summary List of Holdees as against the reported Annex X – Report of Physical Count of Investment Properties.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the officials concerned during the town fiesta to account the proceeds of the raffle tickets and submit a copy presented in the FUR/SOE along with the Statement of Budgeted Expenses for transparency purposes. Henceforth, we also recommend that custodians of any form arising from official activities of the Municipality to turnover the collections made to the Municipal Treasurer for proper accounting thereof in compliance with Item 1 of Sections 68 and 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and Items a, d and e of Section 305 of the Republic Act No. 7160.

We recommend that the In Charge Office (a) recognize and record the differences noted in the Accumulated Depreciation as of December 31, 2023 totalling P601,307.00 in absolute value; and (b) thoroughly review the lapsing schedule and strictly follow the depreciation policy outlined in the notes to the financial statements to avoid inaccuracy.

We, therefore, recommend that the Local Chief Executive comply the rules setforth by CSC and ORAOHRA by immediately hiring an accredited Certified Public Accountant to sit as the Municipal Accountant, if possible. If not, they may contract an accredited CPA to prepare and sign the Financial Statements of the municipality to avoid the question of its validity. Consequently, they may designate a personnel who have financial competence or skill to assume the office for timely submission of the required accounting reports to COA.

We recommend that the Management account and reconcile the unrecorded buildings arising from the Build Operate Transfer (BOT) scheme vis-à-vis with the buildings stated in the market code without prejudice to the legal processes to be undertaken prior to recognition of said properties in the books considering that the LGU has commenced an increase in market rentals which is an indication that the LGU assumes ownership of the BOT buildings in compliance with Sections 58 and 111 of P.D. No. 1445.
We recommend that the Municipal Engineer initiate the application of asphalt sealant at the weakened joints of the project in order to maintain the strength of the concrete pavement.
We recommend that the Municipal Engineer initiate the reapplication of sealing/sealed connections of the PVC pipe particularly the connection of the PVC pipe to the septic tank.
We recommend that the Municipal Engineer inform the contractor to carry out pressurized concrete sealant application and other proper remedial measures to preserve the strength of the pavement.
We recommend that the Municipal Engineer initiate the removal of the used forms (plyboard) in compliance with the result of inspection.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to: (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of the following accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team by setting timelines of submission from Treasury Office to Accounting Office. Likewise, maintain processing and payment of transactions with complete supporting documents. Without doing so, delays will affect both the Treasury Office and the Accounting Office on submission of accounts.

We, therefore, recommend that the Municipal Accountant: a.) make December 20 the cut-off date for the submission of its liquidation to the source agency; and b.) make the necessary adjustments to arrive at reconciled balances for the year ended December 31, 2023 with the source agency.

We reiterate our recommendation that the: a.Local Chief Executive ensure the optimum utilization of the 20% DF by promptly implementing the priority development projects as reflected in its duly approved local development plans and establish the effective and efficient implementation of the projects to maximize the developmental benefits for the intended beneficiaries; b.MPDC (i) initiate close coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant for the proper monitoring of project implementation and the provision of all the necessary information in the Status Report of Government PPAs before submission to the Audit Team. c.Municipal Engineer (i) require timely implementation of PPAs in coordination with the MPDC under their responsibility at all times; and d.MPDC and Municipal Engineer (i) provide the Audit Team joint satisfactory explanation on the delayed implementation of the projects as noted by the Local Chief Executive.

We therefore recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all his Department Heads in charge to observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF. Likewise, the MDRRMO must carefully plan the PPAs to be included in the LDRRMP and strive for their timely and full implementation and strictly utilize the funds according to the guidelines to achieve the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommend that the Accounting Office to: 1. Record the on-going projects in the total amount of P78,361,031.26 in the books of accounts including Accounts Payable. 2. Coordinate with the Engineering Office to establish a reporting mechanism on the status of on-going constructions-in-progress and completed projects to prevent delays in recognition of the same to their appropriate accounts and to recognize the same even in the event of non-availability of actual billings from contractors. This practice is also not in accordance with paragraph 27 of IPSAS I.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), through its secretariat, to submit all contracts, together with all the supporting documents forming part thereof, within five days from its perfection beginning this year to enable efficient conduct of auditorial review before these contracts are forwarded to COA, Regional Office for technical evaluation.

examinations of the books, accounts, and other pertinent records of
any person, partnership, corporation, or association subject to local
taxes, fees and charges in order to ascertain, assess, and collect the
correct amount of the tax, fee, or charge.

continue the regular updating of its GAD data base and the adoption of

from employees’ compensations and payments to suppliers, and remit

including the mandatory employer’s share, and in performing the

Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the
existing rules and regulation on the withholding and remittance of
HDMF contribution from employees’ salaries, including the
mandatory employer’s share, and in performing the required
reportorial functions.

10.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the
existing rules and regulation on the withholding and remittance of

employer’s share, and in performing the required reportorial
functions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue their efforts in resolving the disparities in cash balances as reflected in the cash book, GLs and bank statements; and make the necessary adjustments in the books, as warranted; and (b)	Municipal Accountant to (i) take advantage of the availability of bank snapshots in the initial preparation of bank statements to enable its timely submission; (ii) prepare and submit all the unsubmitted BRS for the seven bank accounts; (iii) cancel the stale checks and revert to the CIB balance the corresponding amount; and (iv) monitor monthly the outstanding checks and timely notify payees of their check’s status, as warranted.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to (a) conduct reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and make the necessary adjustments; and (b) henceforth ensure accuracy in the recording of related financial transactions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MEO to (i) submit to the Accounting Office the latest status report on the above-identified projects; and (ii) henceforth regularly monitor and report on the status of ongoing projects; and (b) Accounting Office to (i) make the necessary adjusting entry in accordance with the latest implementation status of the aforementioned projects; (ii) record the related depreciation expenses; and (iii) henceforth investigate the unidentified CIP account balances that have remained in the in-progress status for a prolonged period of time.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer and Licensing Officer to (i) delay issuance of any permits until the contractor pay situs of tax or the End User of projects within Casiguran to not accept any projects until the contractor pay the aforementioned tax; (ii) be more strict in the implementation and enforcement of the Municipal Revenue Code; and (iii) verify and assess the noted tax deficiencies of the nine identified business taxpayers, and take appropriate actions thereon; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) properly assess and collect the correct amount of tax, fee or charge; and (ii) regularly conduct the examinations of the books, accounts, and other pertinent records of any person, partnership, corporation, or association subject to local taxes, fees and charges in order to ascertain, assess, and collect the correct amount of the tax, fee, or charge.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Local Finance Committee to limit the allocation of funds and incurrence of transactions pertaining to EME/discretionary expenses in future annual budgets within the threshold provided in Section 325(h) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) issue demand letters to officials and employees with outstanding CAs; (b) recommend for the adoption legal remedies, as warranted, to enforce settlement of CAs; (c) consider the option provided for the writing-off of CAs; and (d) strictly monitor and enforce the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Budget Officer to see to it that the current year funds of the LDRRMF meet the minimum threshold of 5% of the LGU’s regular revenue sources; and (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) submit the documents required to support the regularity of the subject disbursements; (ii) ensure proper classification and recording of transactions; and (iii) make the necessary adjusting entries, and properly account for inventories not yet distributed.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) MEO and MPDC to (i) strengthen the project planning processes to ensure the viability of the projects with the view of minimizing if not eliminating the existence of idle funds; (ii) immediately act on emerging concerns that were not foreseen during planning within the context allowed by pertinent regulations; and (iii) thereafter ensure that the 20% DF, or any other government fund for that matter, are optimally used; (b) Municipal Engineer to (i) initiate the imposition of liquidated damages against the erring contractors, as warranted; and (ii) henceforth enforce the provisions of law and penalties stipulated in the contract, on any breach of contract terms; and (c) Field or Project Engineers to regularly monitor infrastructure projects, aimed at making timely interventions in case of lapses in implementation.

10.12	We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) MENRO, MPDC and/or other offices concerned to cooperate in prioritizing the construction of SLF within the Municipality; and (b) MPDC to facilitate the pending purchase of lot and/or equipment related to SWM.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to advise the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the requirement on the submission of the Municipality’s financial reports and transaction documents provided in Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to (a) continue the regular updating of its GAD data base and the adaption of similar mechanisms and tools for the mainstreaming of GAD in government operation; and (b) sustain its faithful compliance with GAD budgetary, utilization and reportorial requirements.

12.10	We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MHRMO and Municipal Accounting Office to (i) coordinate in classifying the unidentified amounts that form part of the unremitted balances at year-end; and (ii) henceforth endeavor to strictly observe the provisions of RMC) No. 23-2012.

14.8	We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MHRMO and Municipal Accounting Office to collaborate in categorizing the year-end balance of the Due to GSIS account; and (b) Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile Due to GSIS balances with the System, and act accordingly on any noted discrepancies.

15.7	We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MHRMO and Municipal Accounting Office to collaborate in categorizing the year-end balance of the Due to PhilHealth account; and (b) Municipal Accountant to henceforth determine the validity and correctness of any unremitted balance of the account, and act accordingly.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MHRMO to (i) coordinate with Pag-IBIG to facilitate the issuance of the Pag-IBIG numbers of employees concerned; and (ii) instruct the members to regularly update their loan records; and (b) Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation with Pag-IBIG to ascertain the correctness of the account balance, and take appropriate action for discrepancies, if any.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to require all the persons responsible/ liable to immediately settle the suspensions in the total amount of ₱4,125,500.00.

1.13. We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and the Local Chief
Executive (LCE) agreed to (a) coordinate with the concerned bank officials to
determine the facts surrounding the closure of the three bank accounts, based on
which, take action in accordance with relevant regulations; (b) exert extra efforts
in locating the documents supporting the reconciling items and adjust books as
warranted; and (c) decide on the balances of three closed bank accounts and
proactively explore available options in clearing the said balances.

2.34. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) inventory
committee to complete the physical count of its properties; (b) Municipal
Accountant and GSO to provide adequate reciprocal records and the prescribed
annexes of semi-expendable properties, and regularly reconcile and update the
same; and (c) GSO to (i) furnish the Accountant with a copy of the documents of
the donated ambulances as the basis of a recording in the books and (ii) ensure
that buildings and motor vehicles are insured with the GSIS, as warranted.

3.14. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal
Accountant to (i) adjust the CIP balance consistenet with the actual status of the
identified project; and (ii) henceforth regularly coordinate with the MEO for the
updated status of the projects included in the CIP account; and (b) Municipal
Engineer to (i) furnish the Municipal Accountant with the updated status of the
nine projects that had long been reported under on-going status; and (ii)
henceforth provide a quarterly status report on ongoing projects.

4.25. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator Officer and Municipal Engineer to (i)
ensure the optimal utilization of the fund (ii) provide clear project
implementation timelines to expedite its procurement; and (iii) ensure that the
PPAs to be included in the 20% Development Plan are procurement-and-
implementation ready to fully deliver its development commitments for the year;
(b) Municipal Engineer to (i) strictly and closely monitor all projects
simultaneously implemented by the contractor to check, verify and evaluate the
quality of work and efficiency of the said contractor, and act accordingly; and
(ii) coordinate with the contractors to comply with the required posting of
information and publicity on PPAs of government agencies.

5.14 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MDRRM Officer to
(a) in collaboration with the MDRRMC, to ensure the maximum application of
available LDRRMF on DRRM PPAs, through judicious planning, regular
monitoring of program implementation, and taking responsive actions on noted
gaps; and (b) ensure that the review process of future charges to the STF are
completed.

6.32. We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) initiate a revision of the
composition of the Solid Waste Management Board, and (b) direct the Municipal
Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) to (i) fully comply with its mandated
functions as delineated in Republic Act No. 9003 and its Implementing Rules and
Regulations (IRR); (ii) review and adjust the Solid Waste Management Plan
(SWMP) every two years, or as necessary, to ensure its sustainability, viability,
and effectiveness (iii) include PPAs that will promote waste diversion in addition to
the existing activities of waste collection and disposal; and (iv) requires the
cooperation of the Barangays in the implementation of Solid Waste Management
particularly the establishment of functional MRF and campaign for proper waste
segregation.

7.16. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Business Permit
and Licensing Office (BPLO) and Municipal Treasurer to (i) improve collection
efficiency where the actual revenue and receipts realized during the year were
low; (ii) verify and assess the noted tax deficiencies of the eleven identified
business taxpayers and take appropriate actions thereon; and (c) Municipal
Treasurer to (i) properly assess and collect the correct amount of tax, fee or
charge; and (ii) regularly conduct the examinations of the books, accounts, and
other pertinent records of any person, partnership, corporation, or association
for the accurate assessment and collection of such local taxes, fees and charges
due them.

8.25 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS to (a)
capacitate themselves on the use of HGDG and PIMME through trainings,
conferences, symposia that are being administered by the PCW, DILG, or other
NGAs directly involved in GAD; (b) observe the timeline on the submission of
GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team; and (c) sustain the Municipality’s
satisfactory performance in promoting gender equality in the workplace.

9.5. We commended the efforts of Management and recommended that the
LCE advise the Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with BIR RMC No.
23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed
period.

10.5. We commended the efforts of Management and recommended that the
LCE advise the Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with the existing
rules and regulations on the withholding of GSIS premiums and loan repayment
from the employees' salaries, along with the government's contributions and
remittance thereof within the prescribe period.

11.4. We commended the efforts of Management and recommended that the
LCE advise the Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with the existing
rules and regulations on the withholding of PhilHealth contributions and
remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

13.6. We recommended that the LCE promptly mandate all persons liable to
settle the outstanding audit disallowances in accordance with COA Circular No.
2009-006, dated September 15, 2009.

1.9 We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the: (a) Accounting Office to maintain separate SLs for all cash accounts of the Municipality and reconcile the same accordingly with the cash control account at the end of each month; (b) Municipal Treasurer to update and balance the Cashbooks daily pursuant to Section 63 of the LTOM; and (c) Accounting and Treasury Offices to clear the reconciling items in their records.

2.13. We reiterated our recommendations and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) aggressively monitor and follow through the final demand letters issued to the persons responsible for the liquidation of the outstanding cash advances; (b) continue the withholding of salaries in case of unjustified failure to do so; (c) recommend for the filing of appropriate administrative or criminal charges, as warranted; (d) refrain from granting additional cash advances if the previous ones remain unsettled; (e) strengthen and implement its internal control system on the grant and liquidation of cash advances; and (f) seek approval for the write-off of long outstanding cash advances in accordance with prescribed procedures.

3.22 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) OIC-GSO to (i) furnish the Accountant with a copy of the documents of the donated ambulances as the basis of a recording in the books and (ii) ensure that buildings are insured with the GSIS, as warranted; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) endeavor to provide adequate subsidiary support documents to its PPE GL balances; and (ii) make diligent efforts to obtain the essential information required to accurately calculate the depreciation for the PPEs from previous years.

4.12 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct (a) all officials concerned to carry out the seven unimplemented projects in CY 2024; and (b) further improve efficiency in the utilization of the 20% DF in the next budget years.

6.16 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) and Municipal Treasurer to (i) improve collection efficiency where the actual revenue and receipts realized during the year were low; (ii) verify and assess the noted tax deficiencies of the eight identified business taxpayers and take appropriate actions thereon; and (c) Municipal Treasurer to (i) properly assess and collect the correct amount of tax, fee or charge; and (ii) regularly conduct the examinations of the books, accounts, and other pertinent records of any person, partnership, corporation, or association for the accurate assessment and collection of such local taxes, fees and charges due them.

7.16 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS to (a) observe the timeline on the submission of GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team; and (b) sustain the Municipality’s satisfactory performance in promoting gender equality in the workplace.

8.5 We commended the efforts of Management and recommended that the LCE advise the Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.

9.5 We commended the efforts of Management and recommended that the LCE advise the Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on the withholding of GSIS premiums and loan repayments from employees' salaries, along with the government's contributions and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

10.4 We commended the efforts of Management and recommended that the LCE advise the Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on the withholding of PhilHealth contributions and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

11.3 We commended the efforts of Management and recommended that the LCE advise the Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on the withholding of HDMF contributions and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

1.11 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to
instruct (a) the OIC Municipal Treasurer to expedite the disposal of all
unserviceable properties to prevent further deterioration and decline
in value, and to generate additional funds for the Municipality; and (b)
the Municipal Accountant and OIC Municipal Treasurer to continue
their commitment to conducting an annual physical inventory of all
properties and reconciling these with the Accounting Office’s records.

3.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct (a) all relevant
officials to execute the three pending projects in CY 2024; and (b)
further enhance efficiency in utilizing the 20% development fund in the
upcoming budget years.

4.15 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MDRRM
Officer, in collaboration with the MDRRMC, to ensure the maximum
application of available LDRRMF on DRRM PPAs,; and (b) Municipal
HRM Officer (MHRMO) to (i) facilitate the submission of the proposal
for the creation of the mandatory positions to the sangunian concerned
and the Civil Service Commission; and (ii) henceforth take appropriate
actions to expedite the hiring of MDRRM personnel in the event that
the proposal is approved.

5.22 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) the designated
MENRO and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to
realign the allocation of fund for SWM toward activities that meet the
requirements of ESWM; (b) designated MENRO to exert additional
effort in ensuring funding for essential ESWM initiatives; and (c)
Municipal Engineer to re-assess compliance of the SLF with DENR-
EMB standards.

6.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) require the (i) Municipal
Accountant to enforce strictly the provision of COA Circular No 97-
002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; (ii)
Accountable Officers to promptly settle overdue cash advances; and (b)
enforce the legal provisions allowing the withholding of salary or
disciplinary action against personnel who unjustifiably fail to settle
their cash advances within the prescribed period; and (c) initiate
necessary legal proceedings, if necessary, against individuals who have
failed to settle overdue cash advances, including those who are no
longer in the government service.

7.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS to (a) continue
the regular updating of its GAD data base; and (b) sustain its faithful
compliance with GAD budgetary, utilization and reportorial requirements.

8.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously withhold taxes from
employees’ compensations and payments to suppliers and remit the same to
BIR in accordance with existing revenue regulations.

9.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to continuously adhere with the
existing rules and regulation on withholding and remitting GSIS
contributions and loan amortizations of employees including the
mandatory employer’s share.

10.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to continuously adhere with the
existing rules and regulation on withholding and remitting contributions
and loan amortizations of employees including the mandatory employer’s
share.

11.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to continuously comply with and
adhere to the existing rules and regulation on withholding and remitting
PhilHealth contributions due from employees, including mandatory
employers’ share.

12.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Accountant to continue her satisfactory review of the fiscal transactions of
the Municipality; and (b) ensure compliance with whatever decision would
be accorded on the disallowances appealed before COA.

1.30 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
(a) inventory committee to complete the physical count of its properties; (b)
Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designee to provide adequate reciprocal
records and the prescribed annexes of PPEs and semi-expendable properties,
and regularly reconcile and update the same; (c) Municipal Accountant to
resolve and adjust the abnormal balance of the Communication Equipment; and
(d) GSO designee to ensure that buildings and motor vehicles are insured with
the GSIS, as warranted.

2.25 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
(a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) stop the practice of allowing private
establishments to collect SEDF; (ii) ensure all collections are directly managed
by her office; and (iii) designate authorized collectors at the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office and entry points who are responsible exclusively for
collecting SEDF from tourists staying at local establishments; and (b) Municipal
Tourism Officer to (i) formulate and enforce implementing guidelines and
procedures for the collection of the SEDF; (ii) conduct regular monitoring and
inspection to ensure compliance by tourists on the payment of the required
SEDF; and (iii) conduct educational campaigns with relevant stakeholders such
as tourism operators, accommodation establishments, and local communities to
increase awareness about the importance of ecotourism and the SEDF.

3.29 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
(a) In-Charge to the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator Office
and Municipal Engineer to (i) provide clear project implementation timelines to
expedite its procurement; and (ii) ensure that the PPAs to be included in the
20% Development Plan are procurement-and-implementation ready to fully
deliver its development commitments for the year; and (b) Municipal Budget
Officer and Municipal Accountant to discontinue charging ineligible expenditure
items against the 20% DF in order to serve the real purpose of the fund.

4.11 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue its
efforts to substantially comply with the guidelines set forth in R.A. No. 10121 on
the appropriation, utilization and accounting of LDRRMF to strengthen disaster
preparedness and response capabilities of the community.

5.22 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) initiate a
revision of the composition of the Solid Waste Management Board, and (b) direct
the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) to (i) fully comply
with its mandated functions as delineated in Republic Act No. 9003 and its
Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR); (ii) review and adjust the Solid
Waste Management Plan (SWMP) every two years, or as necessary, to ensure its
sustainability, viability, and effectiveness; and (iii) include PPAs that will
promote waste diversion in addition to the existing activities of waste collection
and disposal.

6.19 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
(a) Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) and Municipal Treasurer to (i)
verify and assess the noted tax deficiencies of the three identified business
taxpayers, and take appropriate actions thereon; (ii) strictly observe the
submission of complete documents to prove the accuracy of declared gross
receipts/sales, which must be maintained in the permanent files of the taxpayers;
and (b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) use the PIL approach only in determining the
gross receipt of a tax payer if all measures provided under the law have been
exhausted; (ii) regularly conduct the examinations of the books, accounts, and
other pertinent records of any person, partnership, corporation, or association
subject to local taxes, fees and charges in order to ascertain, assess, and collect
the correct amount of the tax, fee, or charge; and (iii) seek assistance from the
BIR if the taxpayer refuses to provide the books of accounts and other pertinent
records upon demand.

7.15 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct (a) all
heads of officers to continue mainstreaming and promoting GAD in the
operation of their respective offices; (b) GFPS, though the GAD M&E system, to
sustain its (i) monitoring of GAD issues and concerns by regularly updating its
GAD database; (ii) evaluation of the responsiveness of the corresponding PPAs;
(iii) assessment of the relevance and applicability of the GAD Code in view of the
evolving organizational priorities; and (iv) providing timely inputs therefor.

8.5 We commended the efforts of Management and recommended that the
LCE advise the Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with BIR RMC
No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed
period.

9.6 We commended the efforts of Management and recommended that the
LCE advise the Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with the existing
rules and regulations on the withholding of GSIS premiums and loan
repayments from employees' salaries, along with the government's contributions
and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

10.4 We commended the efforts of Management and recommended that the
LCE advise the Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with the existing
rules and regulations on the withholding of PhilHealth contributions and
remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

11.4 We commended the efforts of Management and recommended that the
LCE advise the Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with the existing
rules and regulations on the withholding of HDMF contributions and remittance
thereof within the prescribed period.

Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) and Municipal Treasurer to verify and

3.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct (a) all concerned
officials to carry out the four unimplemented projects in CY 2024; and
(b) further improve efficiency in the utilization of the 20% development fund
in the next budget years.

MDRRM Officer, in collaboration with the MDRRMC, to specify the list of
PPAs to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in
its LDRRMFIP and ensure that the use and release of such fund is supported

positions to the sanggunian concerned and the Civil Service Commission; and
(ii) henceforth take appropriate actions to expedite the hiring of MDRRM
personnel in the event that the proposal is approved.

performance of its role as partner in the implementation of national
government projects with the view of promoting efficient utilization of
government funds.

the provisions of RA No. 9003 or the SWM Act of 2000 in appropriating for
and implementing its SWM Program; and (b) rigorously implement the

if warranted.

7.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS (a)
continue the regular updating of its GAD data base; and (b) sustain its faithful
compliance with GAD budgetary, utilization and reportorial requirements.

8.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to continuously adhere to
the revenue regulations and prompt remittance of taxes to the BIR.

10.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office continuously comply with
the proper deductions and timely remittances of Pag-IBIG fund in accordance
with the provisions of R.A. No. 9679.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
The Office of the Municipal Treasurer to take immediate action in accounting the undeposited cash collections in the prior years and deposit intact all the remaining cash collections reflected in the books; and
Management to impose stringent internal controls on its cash collection and timely deposit thereof to ensure compliance with the guidelines in the Manual and avoid or mitigate risks of defalcation, theft and losses.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed that they direct the concerned officials to fully liquidate the balance of the petty cash fund in accordance with the provisions of the manual.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed the following:
The Municipal Accountant to prepare an aging of all cash advances for all funds and provide other relevant information on its schedules and in the Notes to Financial Statements to determine the nature and status of its balances; and
The Management require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately and institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees, by withholding their salaries or any benefit due them.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management agreed the following:
The Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books to come up with a reliable balance of the RPT/SET Receivables and the corresponding Deferred RPT/SET;
The Municipal Treasurer exhausts all means to enforce in their locality the collection of RPT/SET against the taxpayers such as but not limited to sending demand letters and posting of the same in a regular manner highlighting the consequences of non-payment of taxes and the next possible action the taxing authority can do and/or conduct information and education campaign on RPT payment; and
The Municipal Treasurer to proactively forward the list of delinquent taxpayers to the Provincial Taxing authority and coordinate with them for the imposition/implementation of the civil remedies in collecting taxes.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed on the following:
The MEEDO head to consistently implement the sanctions set forth to materially reduce this delinquency in water system charges; and
The Municipal Treasurer forwards a copy of the list of delinquencies to the Municipal Accountant and the latter to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up receivable and income accounts for the delinquencies from waterworks system charges.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed the on following:
The Market Supervisor with the supervision of the MEEDO Head, demand for the immediate payment of the delinquent accounts including surcharges. Otherwise, cause the immediate cancellation of the lease contracts with the stall owners and apply all other sanctions provided for in the Local Revenue Code and the Memorandum of Agreement/Lease Contract; and
The Municipal Accountant draw the Journal Entry Voucher to record the accrued income from market stall rentals and submit a copy to the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed the following:
Conduct a thorough analysis on the reciprocal accounts and undertake the necessary reconciliation of the discrepancies noted;
Make the necessary adjustments/ corrections in the books of accounts as warranted; and
Prepare the corresponding disbursement voucher/s and pertinent documents to replenish the erroneous charging of fund.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed on the following:
General Services Officer to update the stock card by recording all issuances of supplies as reflected in the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS);
Office of the Municipal Accountant to maintain the required supplies ledger cards to facilitate the necessary reconciliation between the stock card; and
Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items semestrally and accomplish the required Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) thereafter and submit the same to the auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
In addition, to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Provide the appropriate disclosures in the notes to financial statements pursuant to Paragraph 47 of IPSAS 12 including, but not limited to, the amount of inventory recognized as expenses during the year; and
Strictly follow the Perpetual Inventory Method by recording first the purchases of supplies and materials in the appropriate inventory accounts, and recognize the corresponding expenses only upon issuances to the end-users.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed on the following:
Management to direct the department in-charge to give priority to the registration of these 18 parcels of land in the name of the LGU of Aurora under the Torrens Title System;
Municipal Assessor and/or the Office of the GSO to sort the documents and provide a copy/list of the Deed of  Sale of these Lands with the corresponding Acquisition Cost to the office of the Municipal Accountant and the latter to record the 17 lands/lots in the book as addition to the Land Account in the FS; and
Municipal Accountant to update the SL disclosures with details that can at least aid the departments, especially during the conduct of inventory, in identifying the land with the corresponding titles.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Inventory Committee to expedite the reconciliation of the records of the Accounting and the General Services Office and submit the RPCPPE;
Organize periodic disposal of unserviceable property to prevent further deterioration and loss in recoverable value, clear out hazardous waste or materials and optimize use of storage spaces; and
Timely submit Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable PPE.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the following:
Property Unit to physically segregate and promptly list down all obsolete and unserviceable PPE with complete data in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), duly signed by the Municipal Accountant, Inspector and Appraisal Committee, and approved by the Head of the Agency or authorized representative, and submit to the Disposal Committee for disposal as early as possible;
Disposal Committee to immediately dispose of all unserviceable/obsolete PPEs using the appropriate mode of disposal as determined, to avoid further deterioration, earn income from its sale, if warranted, and make use of the storage spaces occupied by these items; and
The GSO to submit the necessary documents together with the Official Receipt after disposal, as the basis to drop the unserviceable properties from the books of accounts to come up with the accurate balance of the PPE account in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 89-296 and 92-386.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the following:
a) The Municipal GSO submit the Report on Local Road Network using the template provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and complete the necessary information in every field;
b) The Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, provide the cost segregation of every road network owned by the Municipality per road component for the prior years and submit the data to the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO; and,
c) The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal GSO, disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the required disclosures on the Road Network System.
d.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network and compute for the depreciation of the depreciable component of the Road Network.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
Municipal Engineer submits the required Certificate of Completion of the completed projects and other billing documents to the Municipal Accountant for the adjustment/closing of the Construction in Progress accounts to its appropriate asset accounts;
Conduct a thorough analysis in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to update and correct the balances provided in their disclosures and reports and determine accordingly the status of completion of these projects if still part of the CIP accounts or not; and
Reclassify the completed project into its appropriate asset account, determine/compute the corresponding depreciation expenses and submit the corresponding journal entry voucher (JEV) to the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management on agreed the following:
Conduct a thorough analysis on all recorded payables taking into consideration the recognition criteria and the validity of claims through completeness of supporting documents;
Record the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to reduce the amount of payables if circumstances warrant; and
Make the necessary correction/s in the schedule to provide accurate information on the nature of the payables, so as not to mislead the users of the financial statements.
Further, for the Municipal Budget Office to determine the propriety of the unliquidated obligations including the existence of valid claims on such balances.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
Municipal Accountant to look into the details of these retention monies so as to determine those which are due to be returned to the suppliers/contractors; and
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, together with the procurement unit, to coordinate with the concerned creditors to process their claims, or require for a waiver in favor of the LGU to use the claims as income.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct a thorough analysis on all recorded payables taking into consideration the recognition criteria and the validity of claims through completeness of supporting documents;
Record the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to reduce the amount of payables if circumstances warrant; and
Make the necessary correction/s in the schedule to provide accurate information on the nature of the payables, so as not to mislead the users of the financial statements.
Further, for the Municipal Budget Office to determine the propriety of the unliquidated obligations including the existence of valid claims on such balances.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Municipal Treasurer to remit the collections to the National Treasury/National Agencies concerned, or if not warranted, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to reclassify the collections to their appropriate accounts;
Municipal Accountant to reclassify and record the collections and remittances of Documentary Stamp Tax and other fees to the Due to BIR account; and
Municipal Accountant to effect the proper liquidation of all implemented projects to the concerned agencies and remit the remaining balances of the funds upon completion of the project, or seek authority from the issuing agency for the utilization of the excess funds.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed on the following:
Submit a reconciliation of surplus/(deficit) from ordinary activities with the net cash flow from operating activities in the General Fund, SEF and Trust Fund for CY 2023;
Submit a disclosure on the breakdown of the adjustments indicated in Item d; and
Strictly observe the disclosure requirements in the presentation of the financial statements provided in IPSAS 22, onwards.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
Update all the quarterly reports posted on the different FDP Boards maintained in three (3) conspicuous places of the Municipality; and
Strictly adhere to the prescribed timeline/period of posting of the required quarterly reports for CY 2024 and onwards, as required in the Circular.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly observe the guidelines and procedures in the grant of cash advance for Confidential Funds as prescribed in the Joint Circular.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
Municipal Health Officer to coordinate with PhilHealth on the receivables from the latter totaling ₱164,650.00, as well as the six reimbursement claims in CY 2019, by verifying the actual dates they credited the said reimbursements to the LGU’s bank accounts;
Office of the Municipal Accountant to review and trace the prior years’ bank statements for the receipt of the said reimbursement claims from PhilHealth; and
Municipal Treasurer to issue the Official Receipt only upon verifying the actual credit of reimbursement claims to the LGU’s bank accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to ensure that the rendition of overtime services and the corresponding overtime pay be made strictly in accordance with CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2015.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed on the following:
RHU to make proper and immediate coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accounting Office for recording of received medical items; and
Municipal Accountant to properly recognize the inventory as of year-end of all in-kind donations without available cost in accordance with Paragraph 97 of IPSAS 23.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
The Municipal Accountant to provide correcting entries on the erroneous entries made; and
The GSO to issue Inventory Custodian Slips upon the issuance of procured tools to its end users.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
The LDRRMO to submit to the office an endorsement letter from the DILG authorizing the LDRRMO to conduct the abovementioned activities; and
Strictly observe the guidelines provided in the Circular in the proper endorsement/authorization of subsequent seminars/trainings/conferences to be conducted by the LGU.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to properly observe the monthly report format prescribed in Annex B of the Circular for CY 2024 and onwards;
The LDRRMO to submit the required Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF to the COA office for the months of January to March, and May of CY 2023; and
The LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to ensure timely submission of the monthly Report on Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for CY 2024 and onwards.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed the following:
Maximize the utilization of GAD Funds through implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objectives in which the funds were provided; and
Monitor the implementation of GAD projects and activities to ensure that GAD PPAs are implemented as planned.
