observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to review available records and documents to verify the deposit made by the Municipal Treasurer and once confirmed for the former to record the transaction to correct the balance of the Cash Local Treasury account.

We recommended that management direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the reconciling items in the books of accounts of the LGU and to submit to the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher taking up the adjustments.

We recommended that management require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately and institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees, by withholding or suspension of their salaries.

We recommended management of the following:
Direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough analysis on the receivable accounts to include determination whether the claims were impaired or not and to set up the corresponding allowance for impairment, if there is any;
Record the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries if circumstances warrant as a result of the thorough analysis; and
The concerned office to review of the existing Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) of the loans granted to individual and establish a policy to enforce collectability if not provided therein.

We recommended that management require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to submit the certified list of taxpayers with taxes due and the receivables for the year to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for the proper setting up of the Real Property/Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to retrieve the certificates of completion of all items of PPE where no depreciation expense was provided;
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation of PPE accounts, and record the adjustment; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the required Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC).

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
Direct the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to update the stock card by recording all issuances of supplies as reflected in the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS);
Direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to maintain the require supplies ledger to facilitate the necessary reconciliation between the stock card; and
Creation of an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items semestrally and accomplish the required Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) thereafter and submit the same to the auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended that management direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct verification on other prepayments account to determine whether the purpose which the amount was withheld was satisfied; and
Record the appropriate adjusting and/or correcting entries to effect the results of verification.

We recommended that management direct the Accounting Unit to:
Prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts; and
Strictly observe the accrual basis of accounting in recording its financial transactions.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Accounting Unit to:
Conduct a thorough analysis in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to determine which among the projects in the construction in progress accounts were completed or not;
Reclassify the completed project into its appropriate asset account and submit the corresponding journal entry voucher (JEV) to the Office of the Auditor; and
Maintain index for each type of construction in progress account containing relevant information such as but not limited to (name of project, location, funding source, total project cost, duration of the project, total cost incurred to date & progress billing) for monitoring purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Conduct a thorough analysis on all recorded payables taking into consideration the recognition criteria and the validity of claims through completeness of supporting documents;
Record the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to reduce the amount of payables if circumstances warrant; and
Make the necessary correction/s in the schedule to provide accurate information on the nature of the payables, so as not to mislead the users of the financial statements.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough analysis on the reciprocal accounts and undertake the necessary reconciliation of the discrepancy noted;
The Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments/ corrections in the books of accounts as warranted; and
The Municipal Accountant prepare the corresponding disbursement voucher/s and pertinent documents to replenish the erroneous charging of fund.
We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements.

We recommended that management require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate release of the shares of other LGUs in the proceeds of RPT, CTC and other tax collections and to observe prompt schedule of remittance in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 7160.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
The Market Supervisor with the supervision of the Municipal Treasurer, demand for the immediate payment of the delinquent accounts. Otherwise, immediately cancel the lease contracts with the stall owners and all other recourse as mandated under the Local Revenue Code; and
Constantly monitor the strict implementation of the Revenue Code.

We recommended the following:
The Waterworks System Collector with the supervision of the Municipal Treasurer, regularly provide the list of consumers who are “Due For Disconnection” to the meter reader/disconnector and advise them to strictly implement the corresponding sanctions;
The Municipal Treasurer direct the water collector to prepare notice of disconnection to all users with 2 months arrears to enforce payment and non-disconnection thereof; and
The Municipal Treasurer forward a copy of the list of delinquencies to the Municipal Accountant and the latter to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up a receivable and income accounts for the delinquencies from waterworks system charges.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
The Local Development Council strictly monitor the implementation schedule of the   projects as incorporated in the AIP; and
The Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with the responsibilities provided for in paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2020-1 to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommended that management:
Prepare and submit a one-time Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for the received Donations in-kind using the issued Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS);
Use the form prescribed in Annex E of COA Circular No. 2014-002 for the subsequent distribution of in-kind donations.
Conduct a physical inventory count on the remaining undistributed in-kind items, if any, and reconcile it with the one-time report; and
Strictly observe the rest of the guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-009 in accounting and reporting for the subsequent receipt of cash and in-kind donations during the pandemic.
We recommended and the management agreed to avoid charging to the LDRRM Fund procurement of equipment not solely used for disaster related activities, and observe the guidelines in the Circular.

We recommended and the management agreed that the GAD Focal Person and Heads of the implementing offices:
Maximize the utilization of GAD Funds through implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objectives in which the funds were provided; and
Enhance the identification of PPAs during the planning stage and monitor the implementation of GAD projects and activities to ensure that GAD PPAs are implemented as planned to attain GAD’s goals and objectives.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the GFPS to attend GAD seminars and trainings to update their knowledge and build their competencies in the implementation of GAD related laws and regulations, including the concept and application of GMEF in determining the LGU’s level of GAD mainstreaming and identifying steps to improve its GAD mainstreaming efforts.
We recommended that management assign a separate Responsibility Center and RC code for the GFPS which shall serve as the RC for GAD-related expenses.

We recommended and the management agreed that:
The Municipal Mayor direct the appropriate office to submit the list of all on-going government PPA and those that are to be implemented during the year and to inform the SA and ATL within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted;
The appropriate office as directed by the Municipal Mayor prepare and submit monthly monitoring report on project status; and
The Office of the Municipal Engineer evaluate the maintenance of project status in the project signboard as current as possible and updated in accordance with COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that management through the Local School Board correct the abovementioned deficiencies and strictly comply with the guidelines provided in the Joint Circular on the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget.

We recommended that management through the procuring unit:
Use the updated DPRI on a timely basis in setting prices for the needed drugs and medicines in the preparation of PPMP, ABC and PRs. If no DPRI is available on the needed drugs, a previously awarded ABCs for the related items or market survey must be made by the procuring unit; and
Ensure that the PPMP and PRs include shelf-life requirement, as well as technical specifications of drugs and medicines to be procured to avoid unwanted deliveries.

We recommended that management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all of its serviceable properties with the GSIS, and follow the guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2018-002; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide appropriation for the premiums of such insurance coverage with the GSIS. In case of budget constraints, the MBO may charge such appropriation to the LDRRM Fund of the LGU.
We recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor and the Municipal Accountant be included in the list of bondable public officers of the LGU and be properly bonded with the Bureau of the Treasury.

We recommended that management strictly observe the guidelines on the monetization of leave credits as provided in the Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order (EO) No. 292, and exercise due diligence in granting monetization.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant initiate action for  the
timely liquidation of the  outstanding  unliquidated  fund  transfers
through:


         • Sending demand letters to NGOs/POs concerned.
         • Verification/validation of implementation of projects funded out
           of transferred funds.
         • Demanding return of the fund if found not utilized  or  has  not
           been used in accordance with the purpose these were transferred.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the implementing
school at year-end to refund and/or liquidate the  transferred  funds
immediately by sending demand letters requiring the submission of the
Fund Utilization/Liquidation Report to ascertain that  funds  granted
were utilized in accordance  with  the  purpose  stated  in  the  MOA
executed to support the fund transfer.


We  also  recommended  that  management  revisit  the  Memorandum  of
Agreement to expressly include a periodic report be required from the
IAs and ensure to refund any unexpended balance at  year-end  to  the
Cashier/Collecting Officer who  will  issue  the  necessary  official
receipt in accordance with Item 5.8 of the COA  Circular  No.  97-002
dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the management make follow-up representation with
the Department of Agriculture relative to the conduct of Physical
Inventory and validation of the dispersed large cattles. After which,
require the Municipal  Accountant  to  maintain  the  Report  of  the
Physical Count of Biological Assets (RPCBA)  as  provided  under  the
Manual and  the inventory  of  breeding  stocks  and  the  prevailing
market price for each item as of reporting date.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  continue  to  reconcile
the LGU’s records with that of the DSWD FOII to establish the correct
balances of reciprocal accounts especially as at year-end.  Likewise,
make a representation with DSWD FOII for the amount of P30,035.10,  a
prior years’ balance under Self-Employment Assistance (SEA)  program,
to determine the correct balance and to reconcile the amount as  this
did not reflect in the confirmation balance of  DSWD  FOII.  Further,
provide the audit team a statement of reconciliation showing that the
two books have reconciled as of given date.

1.8 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct
the:
a. BAC, including the BAC Secretariat, to (i) explain the use of Small
Value Procurement (SVP) in the purchase of rapid antigen test
kits and submit the requisite BAC resolution recommending the
use of said mode; (ii) justify why the payment should not be
considered splitting of procurement; (iii) recommend appropriate
mode of procurement for future purchases of meals intended for
whatever purpose; (iv) properly plan procurements; and (v)
henceforth, follow strictly the rules on procurement provided in
the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184;
b. Provincial Social Welfare and Development Officer (PSWDO) to
(i) observe the requirements and procedures on the grant of
Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situations (AICS) set forth in
Executive Order No. 19-0008; and (ii) refrain from doing
disbursement functions in the implementation of PSWDO’s
activities;
c. OIC-Provincial Accountant to (i) ensure that all payments of
financial assistance are supported with complete/proper
documentation; (ii) see to it that payments are made directly to the
creditors to whom these are due; (iii) withhold the corresponding
taxes from procurements; and (iv) secure that the modes of
payments applied in government disbursements are in accordance
with COA Circular No. 97-002; and
d. staff in-charge of processing financial assistance at the Provincial
Governor’s Office to coordinate with PSWDO to ensure
observance of applicable rules and procedures.

2.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the OIC-Provincial
Accountant to continue reconciling reciprocal accounts and to promote
the Province’s commitment towards fair reporting of financial
transactions.

3.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the OIC-Provincial
Accountant and the Provincial Engineer to (a) exert extra efforts in the
proper disposition of the unrecouped balances in accordance with
prescribed procedures; (b) facilitate the refund of excessive recoupment
made on the two identified contractors; and (c) henceforth, coordinate
regularly for the timely recovery of advances made to contractors.

4.26 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Provincial
General Services Office (PGSO) and Inventory Committee to (i) ensure
that the PPE items are properly labelled either before its issuance to end-
user or during the conduct of inventory count; (ii) complete the conduct
of physical inventory count and reconcile its result with both accounting
and property records; (iii) submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team; and
(b) PGSO to (i) properly instruct the designated Property Officers of
their duties relative to the said designation; (ii) set the timeline for the
uploading of data in the system; and (iii) cause the marking of all
vehicles of the Province, in the manner prescribed by law and
regulations.

5.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the:
a. Provincial Veterinarian to (i) require the custodian to prepare and
maintain the WOABSPC with details as to quantity, description of
animals, cost, and fair market value as at reporting date; (ii) ensure
that offspring and disposals are completely recognized; (iii) regularly
review the custodian’s record and submit the corresponding
monitoring and inventory report to the PGSO, Accounting Unit and
Office of the Auditor; and (iv) henceforth conduct regular
monitoring and reporting on breeding stocks;
b. OIC-Provincial Accountant, ProVet Officer and the PGSO officer to
prepare and maintain the required records for breeding stocks and
continuously reconcile the same; and
c. OIC-Provincial Accountant to consistently and timely apply the
prescribed valuation basis for breeding stocks.

6.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the OIC-Provincial
Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to (a) devise measures on how to
ensure continuity of office work amidst the restrictions of the COVID-19
pandemic; and (b) henceforth continue to work on the timely submission
to the Office of the Auditor of the Province’s accounts and financial
reports.

7.17 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Provincial
Planning and Development Office in coordination with all Provincial
Offices concerned to (i) judiciously plan for development projects in
coordination with other line agencies of the government, with the end
view of timely execution of adequately funded projects; and (ii) ensure
that the 20% DF or any other government fund for that matter, are
optimally used; (b) Provincial Engineer to coordinate with the (i) OIC-
Provincial Accountant for the accurate reporting of accomplishments
and fund utilization; (ii) Bids and Awards Committee for the
synchronization of procurement processes with the implementation
targets of the projects; and (c) Field or Project Engineers for the regular
monitoring of infrastructure projects, aimed at making timely
interventions in case of lapses in implementation.

8.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) PDRRMC, in
coordination with the Provincial Budget Officer, Municipal Mayors and
other offices concerned, to (i) identify DRRM-related PPAs based on a
robust assessment of actual needs of the province and its component
municipalities vis-à-vis available funds; and (ii) settle existing and
foreseen implementation issues before the said PPAs are submitted for
funding; (b) Provincial Budget Officer and OIC-Provincial Accountant
to monitor and regularly reconcile records of continuing appropriations
for LDRRMF; and (c) OIC-Provincial Accountant (i) to cause the
immediate transfer to the General Fund of Special Trust Fund that
remained unused for five years; (ii) request from the offices concerned
report on the physical inventory of welfare goods and drugs and
medicines as at December 31, 2021 and recognize the same, if any, in the
books under their respective inventory accounts; (iii) henceforth,
recognize inventories using the Perpetual Inventory System; (iv)
provide the lacking support documents on the identified disbursements;
and (v) ensure that future distributions of assistance in-kind are
supported with the distribution list or similar documents.

9.11 We recommended and the LCE agreed that the PPMIU:
a. Coordinate with the project proponent to ensure compliance with
the Enterprise Investment Agreement, and other pertinent rules
and regulations in the subproject implementation;
b. Oblige the project proponent to immediately address the existing
condition, deviations and deficiencies of the I-Reap and I-Build
subproject vis-à-vis the enterprise agreement; and
c. Thereafter, avoid fund wastage by diligently screening the
capability and commitment of potential/future project proponent
groups of PRDP I-REAP subprojects or similar livelihood projects of
the Province.

10.13 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GAD Focal System
to (a) facilitate the updating of GAD database; (b) coordinate with the
Philippine Statistics Office for the updating of the CBMS; and (c) submit
to the Audit Team a copy of the HGDG checklist on PAPs assessment for
the Team’s review and reference.

11.8 We commended Management on its substantial compliance with R.A.
No. 9003. Thus, we recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the
champion offices in the implementation of ESWM to continue
implementing responsive SWM programs with the end view of
promoting the intents of R.A. No. 9003.

12.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the OIC-Provincial
Accountant to (a) exert effort to verify the balances of reconciling items
to support its validity and take appropriate action to completely account
for the balances; and (b) continue its compliance with RMC No. 23-2007
and BIR TRR No. 10-2008.

13.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) PHRMO in
coordination with the OIC-Provincial Accountant to (i) implement the
courses of actions committed by the Province with the GSIS; (ii) facilitate
the enrolment of the BP numbers of its personnel with the GSIS; and (iii)
regularly update the salary information of all of its employees; and (b)
OIC-Provincial Accountant to regularly reconcile Due to GSIS balances
with the System and take appropriate actions for the disposition of any
noted discrepancies.

14.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) PHRMO to
advise and assist their new employees in securing their PhilHealth
numbers before their assumption to office; and (b) OIC-Provincial
Accountant to request reconciliation with the Philhealth for the
variances existing between their respective records, and take actions as
found appropriate.

15.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct once and for all the (a)
PHRMO to coordinate with HDMF for the issuance of the Pag-ibig
numbers of employees concerned; and (b) OIC-Provincial Accountant to
reconcile with HDMF the records of loan balances of members for their
facilitated disposition.

16.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require (a) all the persons
responsible/liable to immediately settle the suspensions in the total
amount of ₱68,301,299.70 and disallowances totaling ₱131,339.70; and
(b) the Provincial Treasurer to abide by the said COE and collect from
the persons liable the amounts disallowed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to present the current portion of long-term liabilities under the line-item Financial Liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable – Domestic account to provide clarity and divulge information that are not readily available on the face of the financial statements as prescribed in IPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that management immediately correct the noted deficiencies and include the smoke detector devices in the regular inspection or servicing to ensure the safety of the occupants of the buildings owned and prevent the imposition of fines, penalties, abatements or closures.

Effectively and efficiently implement the solid waste management strategies embodied in the SWMP; and

Involve closely the barangays in the implementation of the strategies of the LGU’s SWM Plan as the barangay will be made responsible for the collection of segregated recyclable and compostable materials within its jurisdiction and educate more its constituents in the proper waste segregation.

Management provide personal protective equipment to all collectors and personnel directly dealing with solid wastes and send the concerned personnel to necessary trainings as mandated under Section 23 of RA No. 9003.

The management establish GAD monitoring and evaluation system to effectively and efficiently monitor GAD implemented projects; and

The Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order creating the GAD M&E Team as required under Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 so that GAD implemented projects will be religiously monitored and evaluated.

Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan coordinate for the formulation and establishment of internal guidelines on the granting of transportation, medical, burial, financial and other forms of assistance for the proper and efficient implementation of the AICS program.
Management observe the limit provided under Item 5.4 of the DBM Budget Circular 2004-5A in giving honoraria to the members of the BAC Secretariat.

Management observe the provisions of EO No. 77 and DILG MC No. 2019-82 in the approval of official local travels and the Municipal Accountant check the completeness and propriety of supporting documents attached in claims for travelling expenses.
Local chief executive and other local officials concerned submit justification or any corrective action on the deviation from the provisions of LBC No. 125.
Management specify the PPAs in the AIP Summary Form which shall serve as the basis for preparing the executive budget in accordance with the above-mentioned JMC.
Management observe the guidelines provided under PhilHealth Circular No. 2019-009 in determining the monthly premium contributions of its officials and employees.

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the reports and documents relative to the collections of and payments from government funds to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the Municipality otherwise the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. 1445 shall be imposed; and 

b) Submit justification for failure to submit the required reports and documents despite repeated demands.

We recommended that management provide reconciliation on the balance book balance of the Trust Liabilities - LDRRMF Account with the Notes to the Financial Statements and record the necessary adjusting entries.

a)	Direct the MPDC and Municipal Engineer to judiciously prepare and submit project documents to expedite fund releases and implementation and completion of PAMANA projects; and

b)	Direct the MPDC and Municipal Engineer to constantly monitor project implementation to ensure that projects were implemented in accordance with the government standards and MOA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Provincial Accountant and the Provincial Treasurer to continue their efforts in resolving the disparity between the bank and book records, and adjust accordingly the balance of the Cash-in-Bank - Local Currency, Current Account.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office had already booked most of the reconciling items.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the Provincial Accountant to: 

(a) recognize in the respective fund’s books the unrecorded transactions; and 

(b) henceforth, regularly reconcile the balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to enhance the reliability of the said accounts’ balances.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the:

(a) Provincial Accountant and Provincial General Services Offices (PGSO) Head to (i) account for the dormant inventory balance of ₱426,588.55; (ii) strictly follow the prescribed accounting and storekeeping procedures for inventory; and

observations.managementAction: The PSWDO did not fully comply with the proper segregation of goods stored in the warehouse.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the:

(a) Provincial Assessor to provide the Provincial Accountant with the cost or fair value of the road lots as at the date of its acquisition; 
and 

(b) Provincial Accountant to (i) adjust the valuation of road lots based on the FMV as at acquisition date; (ii) correct the erroneous capitalization of road maintenance expenses and other affected accounts; (iii) restore the value of erroneously depreciated road network components to the extent of the remaining applicable amount as at December 31, 2020; and (iv) henceforth, use the prescribed useful life in depreciating these assets.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to instruct the 

(a) Provincial Engineer to (i) explain why fencing in a portion of the completed FMR was allowed or pass his scrutiny; (ii) request the farm owner/Barangay Chairman concerned to open the portion of the road that traversed his farm for use of the barangay people; (iii) ensure that projects are implemented in accordance with the agreed contract duration and that any work stoppage is covered by an approved work suspension order; and 

(b) BAC and BAC-Technical Working Group (TWG) to meticulously screen the bidders to ensure that contracts are awarded to the most qualified contractor/supplier.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the:

 (a) Provincial Engineer to improve the design of the quarantine facility in the Municipality of Casiguran in accordance with the standards set by the DOH and DILG; 

(b) Provincial Budget Officer to ensure that the reported accomplishments are based on factual data; and 

(c) frontline offices to continue implementing  the necessary measures to effectively respond to the COVID-19 pandemic in accordance with the advisories of the local and national IATF with adequate consideration of applicable laws and regulations.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to instruct the PDRRM Council together with the PDRRM Officer to: 

(a) plan for and implement PPAs that would address risks from both natural calamities and the COVID-19 pandemic; 

(b) maximize the use of its LDRRMF; and 

(c) see to it that future planning of PPAs are duly coordinated with national agencies which are mandated to undertake similar projects. 

We also suggested that the Provincial Governor consider filling-up the vacant positions at the PDRRMO for a more efficient and facilitated delivery of disaster responses.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE instruct the GAD Focal System to 

(a) facilitate the completion of GAD database and updating of the CBMS; and

(b) the PHRMO Officer to assist the new employees in securing their Philhealth numbers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to  require the (a) accountable officers to timely liquidate their cash advances; (b) Provincial Accountant to monitor the status of cash advances and send timely reminders or demands for liquidation; (c) Provincial Treasurer to implement the memorandum  directing the suspension/withholding of salaries of delinquent personnel pursuant to COA Circulars Nos. 97-002; and (d) Provincial Legal Officer to file appropriate charges, as warranted, against those who failed to liquidate their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office did not withhold the salaries of personnel with overdue CA.

observations.recommendation:
a) The Municipal Accountant comply with the specific guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant accounts by preparing ageing of dormant receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers on a quarterly basis (Annex 1-3) to support the request for write-off, and indicate in the remarks column the existence of the applicable conditions: a) Absence of records or documents to validate/support the claim and/or unreconciled reciprocal accounts; b) Death of the accountable officer/employee/debtor; c) Unknown whereabouts of the accountable officer/employee/debtor, and that he/she could not be located despite diligent efforts to find him/her; d) Incapacity to pay or insolvency; e) Exhaustion of all possible remedies by the Management to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers; f) No pending case in court involving the subject dormant accounts; and 

b) Comply paragraph 8.0 for the procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the reports and documents relative to the collections of and payments from government funds to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the Municipality otherwise the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. 1445 shall be imposed; and 

b) Submit justification for failure to submit the required reports and documents despite repeated demands.

We recommended that management provide reconciliation on the balance book balance of the Trust Liabilities - LDRRMF Account with the Notes to the Financial Statements and record the necessary adjusting entries.
Require the defaulting AO to restitute immediately the total amount of ₱1.027 million; and to submit the  2891 pcs. unaccounted Accountable Forms and its RCDs.

Submit the following documents for proper evaluation:

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
The Office of the Municipal Accountant:
conduct a thorough analysis on all bank accounts and facilitate the necessary reconciliation between the GL and BS;
prepare the corresponding BRS for the months of January to November, 2020 and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor. To facilitate the preparation of BRS, snap shot can be used if the BS is not yet available;
record the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries as a result of reconciliation and submit to the Office of the Auditor a copy of journal entry voucher (JEV); and
Imposition of sanction and/or disciplinary action on the employee concerned for the failure to prepare the BRS.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately and institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees, by withholding or suspension of their salaries.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
The Office of the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough analysis on other prepayment account and determine whether the purpose for which the amount was withheld was satisfied; and
The Office of the Municipal Accountant record the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries if circumstances warrant.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the non-existing or missing PPE items without available record of accountability be identified and derecognized from the books of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of government agencies to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

Recommendations:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Mayor consider the designation or appointment of a GSO subject to the limitations of existing laws rules and regulations; and
The designated or appointed GSO regularly renew/update the PARs of the PPEs every three years or as soon as there is change in custody or accountability to strengthen control and accountability over government assets.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct a thorough analysis in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to determine which among the projects included as part of the CIP accounts were completed or not;
Reclassify the completed project into its appropriate asset account and submit the corresponding journal entry voucher (JEV) to the Office of the Auditor; and
Maintain index for each type of CIP account containing relevant information such as but not limited to (name of project, location, funding source, total project cost, duration of the project, total cost incurred to date & progress billing) for monitoring purposes.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant:
Conduct a thorough analysis on all recorded payables taking into consideration the recognition criteria and the validity of claims through completeness of supporting documents;
Record the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to reduce the amount of payables if circumstances warrant; and
Make the necessary correction/s in the schedule to provide accurate information on the nature of the payables, so as not to mislead the users of the financial statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant:
Conduct a thorough analysis on the reported Inter-agency Payables to determine the validity of each claim; and
Record the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries if circumstances warrant.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to present the current portion of long term liabilities under the line item Financial Liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable – Domestic account to provide clarity and divulge information that are not readily available on the face of the financial statements as prescribed in IPSAS No. 1.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Conduct a thorough analysis of the reciprocal accounts and undertake the necessary reconciliation of the discrepancy noted;
Make the necessary adjustments/ corrections in the books of accounts as warranted; and
Prepare the corresponding disbursement voucher/s and pertinent documents to replenish the erroneous charging of expenditures.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management comply with the reporting guidelines of COA Circular No. 2015-008, as follows:
The General Services Officer, at the end of the accounting period, render a Report on Local Road Network;
The Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the total road network system; and
The Inventory Committee prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management revert to the GF the LDRRMF balances in the TF account which remained unutilized for five years from the date of transfer to be made available for implementation of programs, projects or activities for other social services.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management immediately correct the noted deficiencies and include the smoke detector devices in the regular inspection or servicing to ensure the safety of the occupants of the Buildings owned and prevent the imposition of fines, penalties, abatements or closures.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the SWMB:
Effectively and efficiently implement the solid waste management strategies embodied in the SWMP; and
Involve closely the barangays in the implementation of the strategies of the LGU’s SWM Plan as the barangay will be made responsible for the collection of segregated recyclable and compostable materials within its jurisdiction and educate more its constituents in the proper wastes segregation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management provide personal protective equipment to all collectors and personnel directly dealing with solid wastes and send the concerned personnel to necessary trainings as mandated under Section 23 of RA No. 9003.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) with the help of the Municipal Planning and Development Office to:
Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation;
Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and
Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.

Recommendations:
We recommended that:
The management establish GAD monitoring and evaluation system to effectively and efficiently monitor GAD implemented projects; and
The Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order creating the GAD M&E Team as required under Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 so that GAD implemented projects will be religiously monitored and evaluated.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management:
Develop a set of guidelines for the effective collection of the Livelihood Cash Loan Assistance;
Strengthen monitoring controls to ensure that the cash provided were used in accordance with the project; and
Enhance monitoring and evaluation for the sustainability of the livelihood programs.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan coordinate for the formulation and establishment of internal guidelines on the granting of transportation, medical, burial, financial and other forms of assistance for the proper and efficient implementation of the AICS program.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
The Municipal Mayor direct the appropriate office to inform the SA and ATL within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted;
The appropriate office as directed by the Municipal Mayor prepare and submit monthly monitoring report on project status; and
The Office of the Municipal Engineer evaluate the maintenance of project status in the project signboard as current as possible and updated in accordance with COA Circular 2013-004.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer:
Issue an executive order creating the CPES – IU, identifying its members and responsibilities or integrate the CPEs function in an existing unit with similar functions which shall act as the CPES – IU; and
Formulate and adopt specific Implementing Guidelines for the evaluation of project types which do not have specific CPES Guidelines.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the BAC:
Use Direct Retail Purchase method in the procurement of its fuel, oil and lubricants and include the estimated amounts to be procured under such modality in the agency’s Annual Procurement Plan, including provisions for foreseeable emergencies based on historical records;
Issue resolutions relating to procurements to be made under Direct Retail Purchase and designate specific officials for the conduct of said modality, as situations may arise; and
Observe the rest of the guidelines set forth in the RA No. 9184, in the procurement of goods and services, particularly fuel, oil and lubricants.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management observe the modes of procurement provided under the Revised IRR of RA 9184 even in transactions which are immediately necessary. All procurement activities must be in accordance with the specific law, rules and regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management observe the limit provided under Item 5.4 of the DBM Budget Circular 2004-5A in giving honoraria to the members of the BAC Secretariat.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management observe the provisions of EO No. 77 and DILG MC No. 2019-82 in the approval of official local travels and the Municipal Accountant check the completeness and propriety of supporting documents attached in claims for travelling expenses.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the local chief executive and other local officials concerned submit justification or any corrective action on the deviation from the provisions of LBC No. 125.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management specify the PPAs in the AIP Summary Form which shall serve as the basis for preparing the executive budget in accordance with the above-mentioned JMC.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management observe the guidelines provided under PhilHealth Circular No. 2019-009 in determining the monthly premium contributions of its officials and employees.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
The HRMO and the Office of the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the payroll summary submitted by the payroll in-charge from the HRMO to ensure that the prescribed threshold of P5,000.00 and above, net of all deductions enumerated and prioritized in the law would be observed;
The HRMO orient the payroll in-charge concerned on the priority of deductions for payroll prescribed in Section 47, Volume 116, Number 1 of the General Appropriations Act of 2020; and
The payroll in-charge, return any loan payments/other contributions deducted from the payroll to the debtor-employee that would result in the further reduction of his/her payroll to below the P5,000.00 threshold upon review of the employee’s submitted payroll summary.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the MEEO shall prepare a 5-Year Business Plan (BP) for each existing LEE in order to determine whether each LEE is still operative and income-generating, or should instead be advised for service shedding, closure, or divestment. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare Financial Reports for each LEE, separate and distinct from the GF.
We recommended that management ensure that the rendition of overtime services and the corresponding overtime pay be made strictly in accordance with CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2015.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall ensure compliance with the responsibilities provided for in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 particularly on the submission of the quarterly status of information and publicity of the PAPs of the municipality for CY 2019.

We recommended that management prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Annex B) for evaluation and verification within the prescribed period set.

a) Use NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 as a guide in the formulation of the LDRRMFIP programs/ projects/activities using  the four (4) thematic areas as mandated in R.A. No. 10121; and 

b) Review and update regularly the LDRRMFIP to consider current vulnerabilities that may arise.
We recommended that management transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF in the total amount of ₱13.127 million to the Special Trust Fund of the Trust Fund.

a) Comply with the minimum requirements to  establish a resilient evacuation center pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 122 series of 2018 dated August 8, 2018; and 

b) Require the Engineering Office in any succeeding projects for the construction of evacuation centers to make plans and specifications in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 122 series of 2018 dated August 8, 2018.

We recommended that management direct the members of the LCPC to prepare and identify PPAs that can help advocate the institutionalization of children’s rights, plans, and initiates/recommends interventions pursuant to DILG MC No. 2012-120 in order to strengthen the LCPC.

We recommended to the management to strictly follow the JC No. 1, s. 2017 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG in the preparation and presentation of SEF budget using SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 (Annex A) and based it on the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP in the implementation of ECCD Program, kindergarten, elementary and secondary, formal and non-formal education programs.

Strictly comply with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA and other applicable rulings relative to the submission of the approved GAD Plan and Budget and the preparation of the Annual GAD Accomplishment Reports to be approved by the different offices concerned and expenditures should be limited to the amount approved in the GAD Plan and Budget to ensure that all planned projects and activities are implemented.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System:  to  Require the Sanggunian to formulate and pass a local GAD Code or Ordinances with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization of the Magna Carta of Women and to ensure the GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans.

We recommended that the Municipality comply with the provisions of R.A. 9679 and limit the HDMF Contribution-Government Share at P100.00 per month for each employee-member, in absence of any agreement that the LGU may increase its employer’s share up to a certain limit. However, the employees may continue to contribute more than P100.00 provided that their Net Take Home Pay shall not be less than P5,000.00.  If in case the Management agree to increase its counterpart, a fixed or maximum amount must be set-up regardless of the amount of employees share for purposes of just and equitable.
observations.managementAction: The management will comply with the provisions of RA 9679 in the year 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive thru the Solid Waste Management Board facilitate the speedy siting and establishment of a sanitary landfill to conform to the requirements of Sections 40 and 41 of RA 9003 in order to achieve an effective solid waste disposal system for the Municipality without prejudice to the administrative sanction provided in Section 50 of R.A. No. 9003.  Likewise, instruct the MENRO-Designate to furnish the audit team a copy of document showing that the Municipality budgeted an amount of P3,000,000.00 from its CY2018 Annual Budget in support to the LGU’s AAPSI submitted last June 20, 2017.

We recommended to the management the following: 2.) We recommended management to prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Annex B) for evaluation and verification within the prescribed period set.

We recommended to management the following: 1.) Review and update regularly the LDRRMFIP to consider current vulnerabilities that may arise; and 2.) Implement optimally the programs, projects and activities contained in the LDRRMF Investment Plan;

We recommended to management to conduct reconciliation on the balance book balance of the Trust Liabilities - LDRRMF Account with the Notes to the Financial Statements and record the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System: 1.) We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System to Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Section 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

Recommendation:
1.10 We recommended the following: 1.) The Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare the respective BRS for the period covering CY 2019 to support the balance of Cash in Bank – LCCA in the Financials Statement as at December 31, 2019 and submit the same to the auditor concerned; 2.) Thereafter, the respective monthly bank reconciliation statements shall be submitted within the reglementary period prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 together with its supporting documents. Snapshot of bank records can be used as substitute while waiting for the monthly bank statement to facilitate the early submission of the BRS; and 3.) Institute possible sanction(s) for the continuous failure to comply with the requirement.

Recommendation:
2.6  We recommended the following: 1.) refrain from granting additional cash advances to Accountable Officer with previous unliquidated cash advances; 2.) enforce full liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary period provided under COA Circular No.97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and 3.) demand the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to effect immediate settlement of cash advances, otherwise, the necessary sanction for its settlement as provided for in paragraph 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 shall be enforced; and 4.) consider the filing of request for write-off of dormant accounts as provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if warranted.

Recommendation:
3.7  We recommended that transfer of funds from one account to another or from one fund to another be accompanied with an approved resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan authorizing such transfer.

Recommendation:
4.4  We recommended the following, to wit:  1.) The Municipal Accountant comply with the specific guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant accounts by preparing ageing of dormant receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers on a quarterly basis (Annex 1-3) to support the request for write-off, and indicate in the remarks column the existence of the applicable conditions: a) Absence of records or documents to validate/support the claim and/or unreconciled reciprocal accounts; b) Death of the accountable officer/employee/debtor; c) Unknown whereabouts of the accountable officer/employee/debtor, and that he/she could not be located despite diligent efforts to find him/her; d) Incapacity to pay or insolvency; e) Exhaustion of all possible remedies by the Management to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers; f) No pending case in court involving the subject dormant accounts; and 2.) Comply paragraph 8.0 for the procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Recommendation:
5.5  We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:  1.) Set-up depreciation expense for the corresponding years where depreciation was not recognized and the corresponding JEV of the same to effect the adjustments in the ensuing year; and 2.) Maintain PPELC of each PPE to record acquisition, description, custody, estimated useful life, depreciation, disposal and other information about the PPE.

Recommendation:
6.6  We recommended that management:  1.) Create an inventory committee to conduct a physical count of the properties of the municipality and submit the Inventory Report to the Municipal Account and to the Office of the Auditor; 2.) Designate property custodians in each department/section to initially conduct the physical count of the property and equipment to facilitate the complete inventory of all PPE of the municipality; 3.) Municipal Accountant and GSO/Municipal Treasurer maintain ledgers and stock and property cards, respectively. Also, maintain a PPE Register to facilitate the conduct of year-end physical inventory, as basis in the conduct and reconciliation of the accounts; and 4.) Submit a copy of the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31st of each year as provided in Volume II, NGAS to facilitate verification of the inventory report.

Recommendation:
7.11 We recommended the following: 1.) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the reports and documents relative to the collections of and payments from government funds to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the Municipality otherwise the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. 1445 shall be imposed; and 2.) Submit justification for failure to submit the required reports and documents despite repeated demands.

Recommendation:
8.3 We recommend that management: 1.) Provide notice of deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from deliveries of goods/items delivered; and 2.) Submit Report of Inspection of Consumables within twenty four (24) hours.

Recommendation:
9.6  We recommended that management ensure that the rendition of overtime services and the corresponding overtime pay be made strictly in accordance with CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2015.

Recommendation:
10.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall ensure compliance with the responsibilities provided for in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 particularly on the submission of the quarterly status of information and publicity of the PAPs of the municipality for CY 2019.

Recommendation:
11.2 We recommended the following: 1.) Require the contractor, at his own expense, to redo/remove and replace the pavement with major scaling; 2.) Require the Engineering Office to investigate and report as to the possible causes for the defect to ensure that the replacement work will be properly executed and effectively serve the purpose; 3.) Require the contractor, at his own expense, to apply epoxy mortar on all pavement blocks with minor scaling; and 4.) Require the Engineering Office to monitor the defective areas and if further deterioration occurs within the warranty period, redo/remove and replace the affected areas, likewise at the contractor’s expenses.

Recommendation:
12.6  We recommended the following:  1.) The Local Development Council monitor the implementation of the projects as incorporated in the AIP; 2.)The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the departments concerned to fast track the implementation of the projects; and 3.) The Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with the responsibilities provided for in paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1 to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

Recommendation:
13.4 We recommended to management that in the formulation of PPAs to be funded by the 20% Development Fund, that every PPAs to be identified should be specific or with sufficient detail, so that no further authorization is required, the appropriation ordinance already being sufficient.  In the case of the identified PPAs in generic terms, specific approval by the Sanggunian is required during its implementation.

Recommendation:
14.5 We recommended the following:  1.) Impose liquidated damages to the concerned contractor; 2.) Forfeit the contractor’s performance security and takeover the prosecution of the project or award the same to a qualified contractor through negotiated contract; and 3.) The Procuring Entity may rescind or terminate the contract, without prejudice to other courses of action and remedies available under the circumstances.

Recommendation:
15.6 We recommended that management prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Annex B) for evaluation and verification within the prescribed period set.

Recommendation:
16.3  We recommended the following:  1.) Use NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 as a guide in the formulation of the LDRRMFIP programs/projects/activities using  the four (4) thematic areas as mandated in R.A. No. 10121; and 2.) Review and update regularly the LDRRMFIP to consider current vulnerabilities that may arise;

Recommendation:
17.4  We recommended that management transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF in the total amount of  P13,127,377.27 to the Special Trust Fund of the Trust Fund.

Recommendation:
18.2 We recommended that management provide reconciliation on the balance book balance of the Trust Liabilities - LDRRMF Account with the Notes to the Financial Statements and record the necessary adjusting entries.

Recommendation:
19.4 We recommended the following: 1.) Comply with the minimum requirements to  establish a resilient evacuation center pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 122 series of 2018 dated August 8, 2018; and 2.) Require the Engineering Office in any succeeding projects for the construction of evacuation centers to make plans and specifications in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 122 series of 2018 dated August 8, 2018.

Recommendation:
20.4 We recommended that management direct the members of the LCPC to prepare and identify PPAs that can help advocate the institutionalization of children’s rights, plans, and initiates/recommends interventions pursuant to DILG MC No. 2012-120 in order to strengthen the LCPC.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a detailed analysis on the RPT/SET Receivables and its corresponding reciprocal accounts. Explain the deficiencies noted and record the adjusting entries pertaining to the unreconciled RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET accounts and take up the correction of the differences noted, if warranted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management do the following:

Require the BAC/MPDC to observe Section 20.1 and 20.2 of the IRR of R.A. 9184 and justify the non-submission of minutes of the pre-procurement conference duly certified by the BAC Secretariat together with attendance sheets for the pre-procurement of infrastructure projects goods with an Approved Budget for the Contract of more than P5,000,000.00. If said conference has been conducted, submit proof that pre-procurement conference is indeed programmed to be an integral part of the projects bidding process for above P5,000,000.00.

Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to defer processing payments to contractors with incomplete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with all the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: In all procurement related activities, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee in coordination with the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator follow strict compliance with the provisions of RA 9184 and the revised IRR, specifically on posting of the Notice of Award and the Notice to Proceed with the Approved Contract to PhilGEPS and issue certification on the posting of the aforementioned documents in any conspicuous in the premises of the Procuring Entity to promote transparency and efficiency in the conduct of procurement procedures.  Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the printout copy of postings of the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed in the PhilGEPS website is included among the supporting documents for payment of procured goods, infrastructure projects and consulting services.
observations.managementAction: The management complied all the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) require all winning bidders of goods, infrastructure projects and consulting services to submit performance securities within the period as stipulated in the Notices of Award and prior to the signing of the contract in adherence to Section 39.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.  Otherwise, consider the forfeiture of their bid securities as provided for in Section 40.1 of the same IRR and to enforce Bid Securing Declaration by imposing penalties as provided for in GPPB Resolution No. 15-2014 dated June 20, 2014, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The management complied the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the Certificate of Acceptance on completed projects be issued after the lapse of the defects liability period, and after all defects and failures, if any have been repaired by the contractor and inspected by the Municipal Engineer or one year from project completion up to final acceptance of the project in order to hold the contractor for the repair of defects pursuant to Section 62.2.2 of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Performance Security of the Contractor be made to remain valid until the issuance by the Mayor of the final Certificate of Acceptance.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with all the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC and Municipal Accountant make representation with the concerned contractors to post warranty securities for the infrastructure projects that had already been fully paid. Henceforth, strictly observe the mandatory posting of warranty security to guarantee that the contractor shall perform his responsibilities as prescribed in Section 62.2.3.1 of IRR of RA 9184.  Otherwise, perpetual disqualification from participating in any public bidding will be imposed because of the contractors’ deliberate non- compliance of the provision of the General Condition of the Contract they have entered into with the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The management strictly observed the provision of Sec. 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the contractors to post Warranty Security in the amount provided under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Act (RA 9184) in the procurement of infrastructure projects in order to fully protect the interest of the procuring agency in case of Structural Defects and/or Failure.  Likewise, the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant should maintain a list of completed projects as a reference for posting the required warranty security.
observations.managementAction: The management complied the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer, in coordination of the General Services Officer, submit to the Audit Team proofs of posting on the programs/projects undertaken in CY 2018, if any.  Likewise, ensure full compliance on the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 relative to the information and publicity of the Programs/Projects/Activities implemented by the Municipality, their monthly updates and the display of signboards in conspicuous places within the location of the project regardless of nature, irrespective of amount.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the provisions of COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality require the contractor an explanation of supplying an inappropriate retention money bond and provide for the correct retention bond in all contents.  Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all disbursements for the release of retention money bond be supported with appropriate/complete documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The management strictly complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant affix his signature/certification on the computation of the HRMO of the terminal leave benefits of officials and employees to signify that computation was properly reviewed by the Accounting Unit as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant implement the monthly net take home pay for each employee pursuant to Section 48 of General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act (GAA) for FY 2018 also known as Republic Act No. 10964 and comply for the succeeding GAA issuances to maintain the required net take home pay as it changes from time to time.

Likewise, we recommended that the Authorized Approving Officer (AAO) for various loans coordinate with the Municipal Accountant before endorsing loans to consider the loan applicant’s Certificate of Net Pay duly certified by the Municipal Accountant in order to avoid instances wherein pay slips are modified in order to make his/her net pay appear to have increased to accommodate additional loan deductions in cases of applying of a specific loan.
observations.managementAction: The management strictly complied with the audit recommendations in compliance with Section 48 of GAA of 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund by speeding up the implementation of the prioritized programs, projects and activities funded out of the 20% DF as per Annual Investment Plan and that closer coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring be undertaken to achieve the desired level of outcomes.

Instruct the Municipal Planning Development Officer to immediately look into the unimplemented developmental projects as embodied in the Annual Investment Plan.  Identify causes of non-implementation and develop feasible and specific solutions to effectively implement developmental projects.

Instruct the Municipal Engineer to be very prudent, meticulous, conscientious and precise in evaluating the timely and perfect implementation of each and every project.  Likewise, he should also conduct regular inspection of the projects so that accomplishment is monitored and evaluated based on the project implementation schedule as stated in the Annual Investment Plan.

Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit monthly to the Audit Team a detailed Statement of Appropriations, Obligations and Balances, showing, among others, the individual programs, projects and activities funded with current and continuing appropriations under the 20% Development Fund and review the balances of current and continuing appropriations for every end of the year for possible inclusion in the annual budget preparation. Unexpended balances of appropriations of projects, programs and activities which were already completed/accomplished should be re-appropriated for other PPAs of the ensuing year upon recommendation of the Local Chief Executive and approval of the Sanggunian Bayan.
observations.managementAction: The management: ensured the proper implementation of the 20% DF projects and to attain fully under DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum No. 2017-1 dated February 27, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management optimally use the 70% allocation of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund through the full implementation of the projects and activities allowed and mentioned in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks.

Increase the collection of the real property tax in order to increase appropriation of the discretionary fund; and 3.) Utilized the funds within the allocated budget or appropriation.

In all procurement related activities, we recommended that the  Bids
and Awards Committee in coordination  with  the  Municipal  Planning
Development Coordinator follow strict compliance with the provisions
of RA 9184 and the revised  IRR,  specifically  on  posting  of  the

to  PhilGEPS  and  issue  certification  on  the  posting   of   the
aforementioned documents in any conspicuous in the premises  of  the
Procuring Entity to  promote  transparency  and  efficiency  in  the
conduct of procurement procedures.

the printout copy of postings of the Notices of Award and Notices to
Proceed in the PhilGEPS website is  included  among  the  supporting
documents for payment of procured goods, infrastructure projects and
consulting services.

We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE)  require
all winning bidders of goods, infrastructure projects and consulting
services to submit  performance  securities  within  the  period  as
stipulated in the Notices of Award and prior to the signing  of  the
contract in adherence to Section 39.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
 Otherwise, consider the  forfeiture  of  their  bid  securities  as
provided for in Section 40.1 of the same  IRR  and  to  enforce  Bid
Securing Declaration by imposing penalties as provided for  in  GPPB
Resolution No. 15-2014 dated June 20, 2014, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the  Certificate
of Acceptance on completed projects be issued after the lapse of the
defects liability period, and after all defects and failures, if any
have been repaired by the contractor and inspected by the  Municipal
Engineer or one year from project completion up to final  acceptance
of the project in order to hold the contractor  for  the  repair  of
defects pursuant to Section 62.2.2 of 2016 Revised  IRR  of  RA  No.
9184.  Furthermore, we recommended that the Performance Security  of
the Contractor be made to remain valid until  the  issuance  by  the
Mayor of the final Certificate of Acceptance.

We  recommended  that  the  BAC  and  Municipal   Accountant   make

securities for the infrastructure projects  that  had  already  been
fully  paid.  Henceforth, strictly observe the  mandatory  posting
of warranty security to guarantee that the contractor shall perform his

9184.  Otherwise, perpetual disqualification from  participating  in
any public bidding will  be  imposed  because  of  the  contractors’
deliberate non- compliance of the provision of the General Condition
of the Contract they have entered into with the LGU.

We recommended that  Management  require  the  contractors  to  post
Warranty Security in the amount provided under Section  62.2.3.3  of
the Revised Implementing Rules and  Regulations  of  the  Government
Procurement Act (RA  9184)  in  the  procurement  of  infrastructure
projects in order to fully protect the  interest  of  the  procuring
agency in case of Structural Defects and/or Failure.  Likewise,  the
Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant should maintain  a  list
of completed projects  as  a  reference  for  posting  the  required
warranty security.

We recommended that the Municipality comply with the  provisions  of
R.A. 9679  and  limit  the  HDMF  Contribution-Government  Share  at
P100.00 per month  for  each  employee-member,  in  absence  of  any
agreement that the LGU may increase its employer’s  share  up  to  a
certain limit. However, the employees  may  continue  to  contribute
more than P100.00 provided that their Net Take Home Pay shall not be
less than P5,000.00.  If in case the Management  agree  to  increase
its counterpart, a fixed or maximum amount must be set-up regardless
of the amount of employees share for purposes of just and equitable.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Planning   and   Development
Coordinator and the  Municipal  Engineer,  in  coordination  of  the
General Services Officer, submit to the Audit Team proofs of posting
on the programs/projects undertaken in CY 2018, if  any.   Likewise,
