004 dated January 30, 2013 relative to the information and publicity
of the Programs/Projects/Activities implemented by the Municipality,
their monthly updates and the display of signboards  in conspicuous
places within the location of  the  project  regardless  of  nature,
irrespective of amount.

We recommended that  the  Municipality  require  the  contractor  an
explanation of supplying an inappropriate retention money  bond  and
provide for the correct retention bond in all  contents.   Likewise,
we  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  ensure  that  all
disbursements for the release of retention money bond  be  supported
with appropriate/complete documentation pursuant to Section 4(6)  of
P.D. No. 1445.

We  recommended   that   the   Municipal   Accountant   affix   his
signature/certification on  the  computation  of  the  HRMO  of  the
terminal leave benefits of officials and employees to  signify  that
computation  was  properly  reviewed  by  the  Accounting  Unit   as
prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Management :

          1. Maximize the utilization  of  the  20%  Development  Fund  by
             speeding up the implementation of the  prioritized  programs,
             projects and activities funded out  of  the  20%  DF  as  per
             Annual Investment Plan and that closer coordination among the
             officials involved in planning, execution and  monitoring  be
             undertaken to achieve the desired level of outcomes.


          2.  Instruct  the  Municipal  Planning  Development  Officer  to
             immediately  look  into   the   unimplemented   developmental
             projects as embodied in the Annual Investment Plan.  Identify
             causes  of  non-implementation  and  develop   feasible   and
             specific solutions  to  effectively  implement  developmental
             projects.


          3.  Instruct  the  Municipal  Engineer  to  be   very   prudent,
             meticulous,  conscientious  and  precise  in  evaluating  the
             timely and perfect implementation of each and every  project.
             Likewise, he should also conduct regular  inspection  of  the
             projects so that accomplishment is  monitored  and  evaluated
             based on the project implementation schedule as stated in the
             Annual Investment Plan.


          4. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to  submit  monthly  to
             the  Audit  Team  a  detailed  Statement  of Appropriations,
             Obligations  and  Balances,  showing,   among   others,   the
             individual programs,  projects  and  activities  funded  with
             current  and  continuing   appropriations   under   the   20%
             Development Fund and  review  the  balances  of  current  and
             continuing appropriations for  every  end  of  the  year  for
             possible  inclusion  in  the   annual   budget   preparation.
             Unexpended balances of appropriations of  projects,  programs
             and  activities  which  were  already  completed/accomplished
             should be re-appropriated for other PPAs of the ensuing  year
             upon recommendation of the Local Chief Executive and approval
             of the Sanggunian Bayan.

We recommended  that  the  Management  optimally  utilize  the  fund
primarily  for  operational  costs  related  to  PFPR  services   by
identifying necessary PPAs that may  be  financed  by  the  fund  or
procurement of necessary drugs and medicines (based on the PNDF)  or
programs/projects/activities  deemed  necessary  and   priority   in
accordance with the purpose of the fund with the intention that will
benefit its intended beneficiaries of the Municipality, in line with
the security of overall health goals.

We recommend to management the following:   1.)  Prepare  monthly  the
Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same within  the  reglementary
period prescribed under COA  Circular  No.  96-011  dated  October  2,  1996
together with its  supporting  documents  such  as  Monthly  Bank  Statement
Snapshot to facilitate the early submission of the BRS, Subsidiary  Ledgers,
paid checks, debit and credit memos and deposit slips; and 2.)  Prepare  the
necessary adjusting entries for any reconciling items noted in the process.

We recommend to management the following:   1.)  Prepare  monthly  the
Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same within  the  reglementary
period prescribed under COA  Circular  No.  96-011  dated  October  2,  1996
together with its  supporting  documents  such  as  Monthly  Bank  Statement
Snapshot to facilitate the early submission of the BRS, Subsidiary  Ledgers,
paid checks, debit and credit memos and deposit slips; and 2.)  Prepare  the
necessary adjusting entries for any reconciling items noted in the process.

We recommend to management the following: 1.)  refrain  from  granting
additional cash advances to Accountable Officer with  previous  unliquidated
cash advances; 2.) enforce full liquidation  of  cash  advances  within  the
reglementary period provided under COA  Circular  No.97-002  dated  February
10,  1997;  3.)  demand  the  concerned   officials   and   employees   with
unliquidated cash advances to effect immediate settlement of cash  advances,
otherwise, the necessary sanction for its  settlement  as  provided  for  in
paragraph 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 shall  be
enforced; and 4.) comply with the provisions of COA  Circular  No.  2016-005
dated December 19,  2016  to  file  for  request  of  write-off  on  dormant
accounts.

We recommend that management:  1.) Create an  inventory  committee  to
conduct a  physical  count  of  the  properties  of  the  municipality;  2.)
Maintain a Property, Plant and Equipment Register to facilitate the  conduct
of year-end physical inventory, the municipality use this as  basis  in  the
conduct and reconciliation of the accounts; 3.) Submit schedule of PPEs  for
the SEF and Trust Fund; and 4.) Submit a copy of the RPCPPE  to  the  Office
of the Auditor not later than January 31st  of  each  year  as  provided  in
Volume II of the New  Government  Accounting  System  (NGAS)  to  facilitate
verification of the inventory report.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant:  1.)  Set-up  depreciation
expense for the corresponding years where depreciation  was  not  recognized
and the corresponding JEV of the same  to  effect  the  adjustments  in  the
ensuing year; and 2.) Maintain PPELC of  each  PPE  to  record  acquisition,
description, custody, estimated  useful  life,  depreciation,  disposal  and
other information about the PPE.

We recommend management the following:  1.)  The  Accounting  Unit  to
conduct a thorough analysis on all recorded  accounts  payable  taking  into
consideration the validity of  claims  through  the  existence  of  complete
documents; 2.) Record the necessary adjusting and/or correcting  entries  if
circumstances warrant; and 3.) Comply paragraph 8.0 for  the  procedures  in
the write-off of dormant accounts pursuant  to  COA  Circular  No.  2016-005
dated December 19, 2016, if so warrants.

We recommend management the following: 1.) The Office of the Local
Treasurer to strictly observe the procedures on the reporting of issuance
of supplies by preparing the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
(SSMI) which contain the weekly consolidated Requisition and Issue Slip
(RIS) and submit the same to the Accounting Unit; 2.) The Accounting Unit
and the Office of the Local Treasurer to undertake the necessary
reconciliation of records at the close of each year; 3.) Undertake the
physical count of inventory items and accomplish the required Report of the
Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to support the reported balance in the
financial statements; and 4.) Provide the required disclosures in the notes
to the financial statements.

We recommend the following, to wit:


     1. The Municipal Accountant comply with the  specific  guidelines  and
        procedures  on  the  write-off  of  dormant  accounts  such  as  by
        preparing  ageing  of  dormant   receivables,   unliquidated   cash
        advances, and fund transfers on a quarterly basis  (Annex  1-3)  to
        support the request for write-off,  and  indicate  in  the  remarks
        column the existence of the applicable conditions as follows:


        a. Absence of records or documents to  validate/support  the  claim
           and/or unreconciled reciprocal accounts;

        b. Death of the accountable officer/employee/debtor;

        c. Unknown whereabouts of the accountable  officer/employee/debtor,
           and that he/she could not be located despite diligent efforts to
           find him/her;

        d. Incapacity to pay or insolvency;

        e. Exhaustion of all possible remedies by the Management to collect
           the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash  advances  and
           fund transfers;

        f. No pending case in court involving the subject dormant accounts.


     2. Comply paragraph 8.0 for the procedures in the write-off of dormant
        accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated  December  19,
        2016.

We recommend that share in the proceeds of the Community Tax and  Real
Property Tax due to the barangay be released within  the  prescribed  period
provided for under R.A. 7160.

We recommend that management:   1.)  Strictly  follow  the  limitation
guidelines  in  appropriating  for  discretionary  fund  and   extraordinary
expenses as provided under Section 325 (h) of R.A. 7160 of two percent  (2%)
of the actual receipts derived from basic real  property  tax  in  the  next
preceding year; 2.) Increase the collection of  the  real  property  tax  in
order to increase appropriation of the discretionary fund; and 3.)  Utilized
the funds within the allocated budget or appropriation.

We  recommend  the  following:   1.)  The   Municipal   Planning   and
Development Coordinator and the Municipal Development Council shall  monitor
the implementation of the projects as incorporated in the Annual  Investment
Plan; 2.)  The Municipal Engineer  shall  coordinate  with  the  departments
concerned to fast track the implementation of the projects;  and   3.)   The
Local Chief Executive shall  ensure  compliance  with  the  responsibilities
provided for in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommend to management the following:  1.)  Strictly  comply  with
the provisions of COA Circular No.  2009-001  dated  February  12,  2009  to
facilitate the review and evaluation process and attention is  also  invited
to the penalty clause provision of said Circular; and 2.) Submit  copies  of
the contract and  its  integral  documents  enumerated  in  Appendix  A  for
contract review.

We  recommend  the  management  to:   1.)   ensure   that   subsequent
procurement of common-use supplies and equipment should  be  done  thru  the
Procurement Service, in compliance with R.A.  No.  9184  and  Administrative
Order No. 17, series of 2011; and 2.) in cases  where  the  requested  items
are not available in the PS-Depot, secure a certification from  PS-DBM  that
such items are not available.

We recommend to management the following:  1.) The  Local  Development
Council monitor the implementation of the projects as  incorporated  in  the
Annual Investment Plan; 2.)  The  Municipal  Engineer  coordinate  with  the
departments concerned to fast track the implementation of the projects;  3.)
In the formulation of the AIP, specifically identify projects, programs  and
activities  in  particularity;  4.)  The  Municipal   Mayor   shall   ensure
compliance with the responsibilities provided for in paragraph 5.0  of  DILG
and DBM Joint  Circular  No.  2017-1  to  achieve  desirable  socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes; and 5.) Optimally utilized  the  20%
Development Fund allocated during the year.

We recommend to the management the following:  1.) Strictly follow the
JC No. 1, s. 2017 of  the  DepEd,  DBM  and  DILG  in  the  preparation  and
presentation of SEF budget using SEF Budget Preparation Form  No.  1  (Annex
A) and based it on the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP in the implementation  of
ECCD Program, kindergarten, elementary and secondary, formal and  non-formal
education programs; 2.) Post the utilization report of  the  SEF  Budget  in
the website of the LGU and/or in  at  least  three  (3)  conspicuous  public
places for transparency and  accountability  in  compliance  with  the  Full
Disclosure Policy of the DILG; 3.) Furnish COA with copies of the  Quarterly
and Annual Reports of SEF Utilization, as prepared by the Local  Accountant,
received by DepEd, the local sanggunian concerned and  Regional  Offices  of
the DBM and DILG; and 4.) In the  preparation  of  the  Local  School  Board
Budget, the members of the Local School Board  give  priority  to  programs,
activities and projects based in the DepEd-approved  SIP  and DEDP  in  the
implementation of the ECCD Program, kindergarten, elementary and  secondary,
formal and non-formal education  programs and  utilize  the  same  to  such
purpose prescribed by law for which the SEF was created.

1.) prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Utilization of  Disaster
      Risk  Reduction  and  Management  Fund  (Annex B) for evaluation and
      verification within the prescribed  period set; 2.) Use NDRRMC, DBM and
      DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No.  2013-1    as    a    guide
      in    the    formulation    of    the    LDRRMFIP    programs/projects/activities using the four (4) thematic areas  as  mandated
      in R.A. No. 10121; 3.) Review and update regularly the LDRRMFIP to
      consider current  vulnerabilities  that  may  arise;  4.)  Implement
      optimally   the   programs,   projects   and   activities   contained
      in the LDRRMF  Investment  Plan; 5.) Transfer the unutilized balance
      of the LDRRMF in the total amount of P13,127,377.27 to the  Special
      Trust Fund of the Trust Fund; and 6.) Reconcile the balance  book
      balance  of  the  Trust  Liabilities  -  LDRRMF  Account with the
      Notes to the Financial Statements and record the necessary
      adjusting entries.

We recommend that the GAD Focal Point  System:   1.)  Strictly  comply
with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No.  2013-01  of  the  PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA and other applicable rulings relative  to  the  submission  of
the approved GAD Plan and Budget and  the  preparation  of  the  Annual  GAD
Accomplishment Reports to be approved by  the  different  offices  concerned
and expenditures should be limited to the amount approved in  the  GAD  Plan
and  Budget  to  ensure  that  all  planned  projects  and  activities   are
implemented; 2.) Require the Heads of the  implementing  offices  to  ensure
that GAD programs, projects and activities are  implemented  as  planned  to
attain the GAD objectives; 3.) Maximize the utilization  of  the  GAD  funds
through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects on order  to
attain the objective for which the funds were provided; 4.) Require the  GAD
Focal Point System to: a. institutionalize the GAD  database  with  adequate
and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which  will  provide  the
bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation;  b.
undertake gender analysis to identify and  confirm  existing  gender  issues
and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to  the
said issues; and c. revisit Annex B of  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  Joint  Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-01 which identifies the data requirements  for  particular
gender issues; 5.) Require the Sanggunian to formulate and pass a local  GAD
Code or Ordinances with the assistance of  the  LGU  GFPS,  to  support  the
localization of  the  Magna  Carta  of  Women  and  to  ensure  the  GAD  is
mainstreamed in local policy-making  and  integrated  in  local  development
plans; and 6.) Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system as  required
under Section 3.4 and 5.1 of  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  Joint  Memorandum  Circular
No. 2013-01.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following measures in order to prevent further accumulation of outstanding cash advances:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
•	  Submit to the ATL the demand letters for the immediate liquidation of overdue cash advances, duly received by the AO/employee. 

•	Refrain from granting additional cash advances to AO/employee with previous unliquidated cash advances. 
•	Comply with the documentary requirements, more so with regards to the Certification of the Accountant concerning the liquidation of previous cash advances. 
•	For dormant unliquidated cash advance, request for write off in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 18, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters were issued to the concerned Accountable Officers.
observations.reason: Observation reiterated in the CY 2018 AAR.  There are still unliquidated cash advances that remain long outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive designate focal person to require the concerned department heads to submit the duly accomplished AAPSI on each findings and recommendations, as presented in Part II – Detailed Findings and Recommendations portion of the CY 2016 Annual Audit Report to COA, in compliance with Section 96 of the General Provisions of 2016 GAA.
observations.managementAction: To submit the AAPSI with comments.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer be directed immediately to coordinate each other and implement the project in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-3 dated May 23, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The projects were implemented in accordance with the guidelines.  Project Implementation schedule were prepared to fast track the implementation.  Project status will be submitted to COA

observations.recommendation:
For the outstanding checks that are already stale,  we  recommended
   the Local Chief Executive to instruct:

   1. The  Municipal  Treasurer  to  send  written  notices  to  the  payees
      concerned to remind them of the  existence  of  check/s  which  became
      stale.    After   which   she   shall   issue    new    checks    upon
      presentation/surrender  of  the  cancelled   stale   check   and   the
      corresponding DVs with supporting documents be returned by her to  the
      Municipal Accountant.  The corresponding amounts  of  cancelled  stale
      checks should be reverted to the book balance.


   2. The Municipal Accountant to furnish the Municipal  Treasurer  and  the
      Office of the Audit Team Leader copies of the Journal  Entry  Vouchers

     a. Submit  a  Report  upon  the  conduct  of  a  proper  and  thorough

        Municipality should request for write-off of the  dormant  accounts
        according to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December  19,  2016  -
        Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off  of  Dormant  Receivable
        Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances, Fund Transfers of NGAs, LGUs,
        and GOCC, provided the ten  (10)  year  dormancy  period  has  been
        reached.

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  and  Local  Disaster
   Risk Reduction and Management Officer prepare and  submit,  respectively,
   the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the  LDRRMF  within  the
   period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002, which is on or  before
   the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of  utilizing  the
   fund for any purpose beyond those expressly prescribed by the above cited
   regulations. Management should ensure that only development projects that
   can contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic  development
   and environmental outcomes of the LGU, and shall partake  the  nature  of
   investment or capital expenditures be  charged  against  20%  Development
   Fund. Likewise, Program of Works be submitted for further verification.

Require the defaulting AO to restitute immediately  the  total  amount
of P1,027,647.02; and to submit the 2891 pcs. unaccounted Accountable  Forms
and its RCDs.

Require the concerned Accountable Officer to submit final  reports  of
her accountabilities to the COA Audit Team.

We recommend the  following  measures  in  order  to  prevent  further
accumulation of outstanding cash advances:

      Direct the Municipal Accountant to:

           liquidation of overdue  cash  advances,  duly  received  by  the

      previous unliquidated cash advances.

         • Comply with the documentary requirements, more so  with  regards
           to  the  Certification  of   the   Accountant   concerning   the
           liquidation of previous cash advances.

    • For dormant unliquidated  cash  advance,  request  for  write  off  in
      accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 18, 2016.

copies of contracts together with all the  supporting  documents  (certified
true copy) to the COA within the period prescribed under  COA  Circular  No.
2009-001 to avoid the aforementioned penalties.

           description  of  the  PPA;  contractor  or  supplier,  mode   of
           procurement; funding source; cost or  approved  budget;  project
           duration including start and completion dates; and location;


        2. Direct the MPDC  to  submit  regularly  the  monthly  monitoring
           reports in coordination with the Municipal Engineer’s Office  to

        3. Notify the public through signboards of their projects  location

furnish the Audit Team with the copies of the  following  documents  on  all
funds transferred by Department of Agrarian Reform as provided for  in  Item

   A. Review of Contracts


   1. Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/ Trust Agreement;

      equipment owned by the Municipality, which was used in the preparation
      of the ABC;
   4. Why include Salary of the Civil Engineer in Detailed Program of  Works
      when it is already included in the 22% indirect cost as per  DPWH  D.O
      #72, series of 2012; and

   1. Variation Order, if any; and

      (CIS)


   1. Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/ Trust Agreement;

   3. Unit Cost Analysis of all aggregates and other construction  materials

      project site.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the LGU’s
records with that of the Provincial Government of Isabela to establish
the correct balances of the reciprocal accounts.

We recommended  that  management  submit  an  amendment  to  the
Memorandum of Agreement  dated  December  11,  2000  between  LGU    Aurora,
Isabela and the Department of Agriculture duly agreed upon by the    parties
concerned, i.e. allowing the  cooperative  to  pay  directly  to  DA-RFU  02
instead of to the LGU.

            Also, we recommended that the  LGU  make  proper  representation
with DA-RFU 02 for the extension of the payment period,  otherwise,   demand
full  payment  of  the  overdue  account  from  the  cooperative  to  settle
its liability to the source NGA.

We recommended  that  management  return/refund  the  unutilized
balances of the fund  transfers  to  the  source  National  Government
Agencies to fully liquidate the same, thus, reduce  the  liability  of
the LGU.

We reiterated our recommendation that the  Municipal  Accountant
ensure timely submission of accounts to facilitate the  audit  of  the
transactions  as  well  as  to  avoid  the  possible   suspension   or
disallowance  of  the  same  and  the  imposition  of   administrative
sanctions.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the
Municipal Accountant and Local Disaster Risk Reduction and  Management
Officer to prepare and submit, respectively,  the  Monthly  Report  on
Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF  within  the  period  prescribed
under COA Circular  No.  2012-002.  Correspondingly,  the  report  for
December 2016 should be submitted for verification.

We recommended  that  management  submit  the  purchase  orders,
government contracts and their supporting documents to the  Office  of
the Auditor within the period prescribed under COA Circular No.  2009-
001 to facilitate the systematic and effective review thereof.

We recommended  that  management  ensure  that  all  procurement
activities be supported with complete documentation as prescribed.  It
is emphasized that complete  documentation  does  not  only  mean  the
submission of documents but also the completeness and  consistency  of
the details thereof.

            As to the disbursements amounting to P29,924.50, the lacking and
the necessary corrective documents should be  submitted  to  ascertain
the validity  and  propriety  of  the  thereof.  Otherwise,  the  said
transactions may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended  that  the   Municipal   Social   Welfare   and
Development      Officer be required to submit the (1) list of children  fed
and   their profile    for the 20 feeding days; (2) menu for the 20  feeding
days; and   (3) corrective/explanatory documents,  if  any,  for  the  other
deficiencies noted     to fully ascertain the validity and propriety of  the
disbursements. Otherwise,    the same may  be  suspended  or  disallowed  in
audit.

            It is emphasized that complete documentation does not only  mean
the   submission of documents but also the completeness and  consistency  of
the   details thereof.

We recommended that  management  ensure  that  all  procurements
chargeable against  the  Special  Education  Fund  be  supported  with
complete documentation as prescribed. It is emphasized  that  complete
documentation does not only mean the submission of documents but  also
the completeness and consistency of the details thereof.

            As to the disbursements totaling P169,785.46,  the  lacking  and
the necessary corrective/explanatory documents, if any,  be  submitted
to ascertain  the  validity  and  propriety  thereof.  Otherwise,  the
transactions may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that management prioritize  the  provision  of  a
sanitary landfill  for  the  LGU  in  compliance  with  the  pertinent
provisions of the Solid Waste Management Act.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Engineer’s  Office  post  or
display a tarpaulin or signboard  for  all  its  projects  at  the  project
location  pursuant  to   COA   Circular   No.   2013-004,   thus,   promote
transparency. Also, the Local Chief  Executive  shall  inform  the  SA
and ATL concerned  within  10  days  after  the  appropriate  signboard
and/or public notices are already posted as basis   for validation.

Recommendation

      We recommend that Management shall require  the  Municipal  Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the  balances  of  the  account  in
their respective books of accounts and to verify any  unreconciled  balances
in the Cash in Bank accounts for all funds  and  to  prepare  the  necessary
adjustments to reconcile the balances per Cashbooks and SLs.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to comply  with
pertinent provisions of COA Circular  No.  96-011  on  the  preparation  and
submission of BRS to achieve an accurate  financial  reporting  of  Cash  in
Bank accounts.

Recommendation
      We recommend  that  concerned  accountable  officers  be  directed  to
submit final  reports  of  their  accountabilities  to  the  Office  of  the
Auditor.

Recommendations

      We recommend that the accountable officers be  directed  to  keep  the
Reports  of  Collections  and  Deposits  properly  organized  and  filed  to
facilitate easy retrieval of  records  for  validation  and/or  verification
purposes.

      Further, reconcile  the  differences  between  the  cashbook  and  the
report of collections and deposits of the accountable officers  promptly  to
ensure the accuracy and completeness of recorded transactions.

Management Comment
      The Municipal Treasurer instructed the collectors to  properly  filled
up their RCD’s and informed that the Report of Collection  and  Deposits  of
each collector were already properly filed. Also,  the  Municipal  Treasurer
promised that clearer copies of the RCD  of  all  the  accountable  officers
will be prepared starting year 2017.

Recommendations

      We recommend that Revenue Collectors and the  Municipal  Treasurer  be
directed to check the number  of  issuances  and  number  of  the  remaining
balances of the accountable forms and to fill-up properly Sections C  and  D
and the verification and acknowledgement column of  the  RCD  by  indicating
the amount in words and in figures received by the Municipal  Treasurer  for
easy  monitoring  of  the  movement  of  the  accountable  forms   and   the
remittances of collections. Further, Annex 41 of the  same  Manual  provides
instructions on how to fill-up the required form.

 Management Comment

      The Municipal Treasurer promised to comply with the recommendation.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the accountable officers/collectors be  directed  to
remit/turnover their collections to the liquidating officer for  deposit  to
the depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 of  the
NGAS Manual. The Municipal Treasurer should set control  measures  to  avoid
or minimize delay in remittances  of  collections  and  impose  disciplinary
action to erring accountable officers.

Recommendation

      We recommend that all Revenue Collectors of the Municipality of Aurora
be directed to submit the RAAF at the end of each month to  the  Audit  Team
through the Municipal Accounting Office.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the  Municipal  Accounting  Office  be  directed  to
maintain an updated subsidiary ledger  of  each  collecting  officers  where
collections are recorded upon receipt of the daily RCD for  easy  validation
of daily collections.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management:

   1. Demand the concerned officials and employees  with  unliquidated  cash
      advances to effect immediate settlement of cash  advances,  otherwise,
      the necessary sanction for its settlement as provided for in paragraph
      9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-004  dated  28  November  2012  shall  be
      enforced;




   2. Refrain from granting additional cash advances to Accountable  Officer
      with previous unliquidated cash advances; and




   3. Ensure the compliance with the documentary requirements, more so  with
      regards to the Certification of the Accountant concerning the previous
      liquidation of cash advances.

Recommendation

      We recommend the following:


        1. The Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer maintain  stock  ledger
           cards, property ledger cards, and other subsidiary  records  and
           reconcile accounting and property records periodically;




        2. The Inventory Committee shall conduct complete physical count of
           all property and reconcile results with accounting  records  and
           effect any adjustments in the books;




        3. The  Municipal  Treasurer/Property  officer  shall  prepare  the
           Inventory and Inspection Report on all unserviceable  properties
           that  will  be  identified  and  counted  and  facilitate  their
           immediate disposal to prevent further deterioration and decrease
           in salvage values. The Municipal  Accountant  shall  effect  the
           necessary adjustment in the PPE Account to reclassify  the book
           value  of  unserviceable  properties  to  Other  Assets  account
           pending  disposal  and  close  the   corresponding   Accumulated
           Depreciation account

Recommendation

      We recommend  that  the  DILG  Officer  be  directed  to  monitor  the
compliance of the LGU of the DILG Circulars mentioned above and ensure  that
postings of the reports were  strictly  followed,  moreover  the  prescribed
forms shall be used. Bulletin boards fabricated must  be  covered  by  glass
and should be fixed in a permanent place to  prevent  the  documents  to  be
destroyed by strong wind and other bad elements.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the BAC be required to prepare and submit  regularly
the Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPBB every end of each semester  as
required under Section 12.2 of the IRR of RA 9184.  A printed  copy  of  the
report shall be furnished to this Office to allow the audit team  to  verify
the status of procurement activities specified  in  the  Annual  Procurement
Plan as basis for auditor’s action, if any.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management furnish the  office  of  the  Audit  Team
copies of contracts together with all the  supporting  documents  (certified
true copies) to the COA within the period prescribed under COA Circular  No.
2009-001 to avoid the aforementioned penalties.

Recommendations

      We recommend that Management submit to the Audit  Team  those  lacking
documents of the  subject  post-audited  transactions  to  avoid  suspension
and/or disallowance in audit.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management furnish the  office  of  the  Audit  Team
copies of contracts together with all the  supporting  documents  (certified
true copies) to the COA within the period prescribed under COA Circular  No.
2009-001 to avoid the aforementioned penalties.

Recommendation

      We recommend that adherence by the Municipality to the  provisions  of
RA 9184 be observed strictly.

Recommendation


      We recommend for Management to  observe  strict  compliance  with  the
requirements enumerated in Section 7 of DBM LBC 103 dated May 13,  2013  and
5.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Recommendation

      We recommended  that  the  Local  School  Board  strictly  follow  the
provisions set under Section 272 of  RA  7160,  DECS,  DBM  and  DILG  joint
Circulars  01  s,  01-A  and  01-B  for  the  utilization  of  SEF  of   the
Municipality

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require all officials and employees concerned and responsible in the preparation, approving of trip tickets as well as authorized passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip and submission thereof, for reporting purposes, must consider the following:

a. Required information/ date in the Driver’s Trip Ticket should be filled-up according and accurately and duly approved by official not lower in rank that the 2nd ranking official in the LGU.
b. As to the distance travelled, to determine  the accurate consumption of fuel:
All vehicles with functional odometer should record the kilometer reading before and after the trip.
All vehicles with non-functional/ defective odometer should indicate the approximate distance traveled including the no. of trips undertaken during the day. Immediate repair of the odometer should be considered.
All heavy equipment and other similar equipment should record the number of hours of operation and duly certified by the supervisor/in charge.
All travels with multiple trips from point of destination and back (especially on hauling activities), the no. of trips should be indicated.
Every vehicle/ heavy equipment should indicate the factor of its consumption for the distance travelled and/or the no. of liters for every hour of operation to determine the actual consumption of the vehicle/ equipment.
c. All required reports should be prepared monthly and regularly.
Require the signature/ approval of all trips made and all passengers should sign the same.

Monthly Report of official travels shall be prepared by the driver concerned summarizing in chronological order his trips for the month. For each vehicle, one report shall be prepared.
Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and shall show, among others the different types of motor vehicles utilizes during the month, total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle. The consumed report shall be submitted to the auditor to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.

All other measure should be exercised by all concerned to prevent wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

We recommend that the accountable officers/collectors remit/turnover their collections to the liquidating officer for deposit to the depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 of the NGAS Manual. The Municipal Treasurer should set control measures to avoid or minimize delayed remittance of collections and institute disciplinary action to concerned accountable employees under her supervision, if necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU comply with the Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9003. The Municipality should adopt a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program which shall ensure the protection of public health and environment.

We recommend that Management

1.Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Commission on Audit at the beginning of the year, the list of the ongoing projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented.The list shall include the project name; brief description of the PPA; contractor or supplier, mode of procurement; funding source; cost or approved budget; project duration including start and completion dates; and location;

2. Direct the MPDC to submit regularly the monthly monitoring reports in coordination with the Municipal Engineer’s Office to the Audit Team. The report should also categorize the projects as to its nature, status of completion, cost and funding source; and

3. Notify the public through signboards of their projects location and inform the SA thru ATL that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

We recommend that Management furnish the office of the Audit Team copies of contracts together with all the supporting documents (certified true copies) to the COA within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to avoid the aforementioned penalties.
